##### exportDocument()

(params: ExportDocumentParams) => Promise<ExportDocumentResult>

Export a document from the remote backend to a local file.  Content retrieval: calls `GET /v1/documents/:slug/versions/:n` and extracts the `content` field. Then serializes and writes locally using writeExportFile.

##### exportAll()

(params: ExportAllParams) => Promise<ExportAllResult>

Export all documents from the remote backend to a directory.  Iterates via listDocuments (cursor-based pagination). Individual document failures are collected in skipped, not thrown.

##### importDocument()

(params: ImportDocumentParams) => Promise<ImportDocumentResult>

Import a document from a file on disk into the remote backend.  Parses the file locally (same logic as LocalBackend), then uses the remote API to create or update the document.

##### getChangesSince()

(_dbVersion: bigint) => Promise<Uint8Array>

Not supported by RemoteBackend — cr-sqlite sync is a LocalBackend feature. The remote api.llmtxt.my endpoint for changeset exchange is planned in P3.

##### applyChanges()

(_changeset: Uint8Array) => Promise<bigint>

#### MeshNotImplementedError

typeof MeshNotImplementedError

Thrown when a mesh-specific sync method is called on `MeshBackend` before the T386 P2P sync engine has been installed.  The MeshBackend stub delegates all standard Backend interface methods to its internal LocalBackend. Only the T386-specific mesh methods (peer negotiation, changeset exchange, etc.) throw this error, so agents that do not call those methods can use MeshBackend today without blocking on T386.  To resolve: implement T386 (P2P Mesh Sync Engine) and replace the stub with the real MeshBackend from packages/llmtxt/src/mesh/.

Members:

##### code

"MESH_NOT_IMPLEMENTED"

#### HubUnreachableError

typeof HubUnreachableError

Thrown when a hub-and-spoke spoke cannot reach the hub for a write operation.  Ephemeral spokes MUST fail fast with this error — writes are never silently dropped (ARCH-T429 §7.1). The `cause` property holds the underlying network error for diagnostics.

Members:

##### code

"HUB_UNREACHABLE"

##### cause

unknown

#### HubWriteQueueFullError

typeof HubWriteQueueFullError

Thrown when a persistent spoke's write queue exceeds the 1000-entry limit (ARCH-T429 §7.1). The 1001st write while the hub is unreachable must be rejected with this error rather than silently dropped or discarded.

Members:

##### code

"HUB_WRITE_QUEUE_FULL"

##### queueSize

number

#### HubSpokeBackend

typeof HubSpokeBackend

Composite Backend for hub-and-spoke topology with `persistLocally=true`.  Routing semantics (ARCH-T429 §5.2 persistent spoke): - Reads (documents, versions, events, CRDT sections, presence) → LocalBackend (replica). - Writes (createDocument, publishVersion, A2A, scratchpad, leases) → RemoteBackend (hub). - CRDT applyCrdtUpdate → RemoteBackend (hub is authoritative); local replica is updated   on next background sync. - Signed URLs, webhooks, org/API-key ops → RemoteBackend (hub-owned resources).  TODO(T449): Implement write-queue persistence in local SQLite (`hub_write_queue` table) so queued writes survive agent restart. Current behaviour: writes fail fast when the hub is unreachable, matching ephemeral-spoke semantics. Track in T449.  TODO(T449): Implement background sync loop (poll hub for new events and replicate to local replica). Current behaviour: local replica is only updated when hub writes are acknowledged inline.

Members:

##### config

BackendConfig

##### local

LocalBackend

##### remote

RemoteBackend

##### _hubWrite()

<T>(operation: string, fn: () => Promise<T>) => Promise<T>

Wrap a hub write operation so network failures surface as HubUnreachableError rather than raw fetch errors. This ensures writes are never silently dropped (ARCH-T429 §7.1).

#### MeshBackend

typeof MeshBackend

Stub Backend for mesh topology.  All standard `Backend` interface methods delegate to an internal `LocalBackend`. This means a mesh-topology agent can do meaningful local work (create docs, publish versions, etc.) today, before T386 (P2P Mesh Sync Engine) is installed.  The P2P sync engine (peer discovery, cr-sqlite changeset exchange, Ed25519 mutual handshake) is provided by T386. Until T386 ships, this stub emits a warning on `open()` to signal that sync is not active. T386-specific mesh methods that are not part of the Backend interface throw `MeshNotImplementedError` with a clear follow-up pointer.

Members:

##### config

BackendConfig

##### local

LocalBackend

##### meshConfig

MeshConfig

##### open()

() => Promise<void>

##### close()

() => Promise<void>

(params: Parameters<Backend["createDocument"]>[0]) => Promise<import("../core/backend.js").Document>

#### HandshakeFailedError

typeof HandshakeFailedError

Thrown when the Ed25519 mutual handshake fails.

Members:

##### code

"HANDSHAKE_FAILED"

#### PeerUnreachableError

typeof PeerUnreachableError

Thrown when a peer is unreachable after max retries.

Members:

##### code

"PEER_UNREACHABLE"

#### ChangesetTooLargeError

typeof ChangesetTooLargeError

Thrown when a changeset exceeds the maximum size.

Members:

##### code

"CHANGESET_TOO_LARGE"

#### UnixSocketTransport

typeof UnixSocketTransport

UnixSocketTransport — primary transport for same-machine collaboration.  - Listens on a Unix domain socket. - Frames messages with 4-byte LE length prefix. - Completes Ed25519 mutual handshake before passing changesets to the sync engine. - Handles reconnection with 3-retry exponential backoff. - Emits `peerError` events for unreachable peers.  Spec: P3 spec §4.2, §4.3

Members:

##### type

"unix"

##### identity

TransportIdentity

##### socketPath

string

##### server

net.Server | null

##### listen()

(onChangeset: (peerId: string, changeset: Uint8Array) => void) => Promise<void>

Listen for incoming connections on the Unix socket. MUST NOT call onChangeset until the handshake completes.

##### handleIncomingConnection()

(socket: net.Socket, onChangeset: (peerId: string, changeset: Uint8Array) => void) => Promise<void>

Handle an incoming connection: run handshake as responder, then receive changesets.  Uses an async generator over framed messages so each async step (signature verify, sign) completes before the next message is processed — no race between async callbacks and socket close events.

##### sendChangeset()

(peerId: string, peerAddress: string, changeset: Uint8Array) => Promise<void>

Send a changeset to a peer. Runs the full handshake as the initiator, then sends the changeset. Retries up to MAX_RETRIES times with exponential backoff.

##### sendOnce()

(_peerId: string, socketPath: string, changeset: Uint8Array) => Promise<void>

One connection attempt: connect, handshake as initiator, send changeset.  Uses async generator for sequential message processing to avoid race conditions between async crypto operations and socket events.

##### close()

() => Promise<void>

Graceful shutdown: close the server and stop accepting connections.

#### HttpTransport

typeof HttpTransport

HttpTransport — secondary transport for cross-machine collaboration.  - Listens on a local HTTP port. - Changeset exchange: POST /mesh/changeset (binary body). - Ed25519 handshake: POST /mesh/handshake (JSON body). - Handles reconnection with 3-retry exponential backoff. - Emits `peerError` events for unreachable peers.  Spec: P3 spec §4.2, §4.3

Members:

##### type

"http"

##### identity

TransportIdentity

##### port

number

##### host

string

##### server

http.Server<typeof http.IncomingMessage, typeof http.ServerResponse> | null

##### sessions

Map<string, { peerPublicKey: Uint8Array; }>

In-memory session store: maps `peerId -> { verified: true, peerPublicKey }`. A session is established after the handshake and used to authorize changeset delivery.

##### listen()

(onChangeset: (peerId: string, changeset: Uint8Array) => void) => Promise<void>

Start the HTTP server and listen for incoming handshake + changeset requests.

##### handleRequest()

(req: http.IncomingMessage, res: http.ServerResponse, onChangeset: (peerId: string, changeset: Uint8Array) => void) => Promise<void>

##### handleHandshakeRequest()

(req: http.IncomingMessage, res: http.ServerResponse) => Promise<void>

POST /mesh/handshake — 3-message handshake over HTTP.  Phase 1 (client sends INIT JSON):    "phase": 1, "agentId": "...", "pubkey": "base64", "challenge": "base64"  Server responds:    "agentId": "...", "pubkey": "base64", "sig": "base64", "challenge": "base64"   Phase 2 (client sends FINAL JSON):    "phase": 2, "agentId": "...", "sig": "base64"  Server responds:    "ok": true

##### handleChangesetRequest()

(req: http.IncomingMessage, res: http.ServerResponse, onChangeset: (peerId: string, changeset: Uint8Array) => void) => Promise<void>

POST /mesh/changeset — receive a changeset from an authenticated peer.  MUST have a valid authenticated session (completed handshake). Request headers: `X-Agent-Id: <agentId>`. Body: raw binary changeset (application/octet-stream). Response: 200 OK with empty body (or delta body in future bidirectional sync).

##### sendChangeset()

(peerId: string, peerAddress: string, changeset: Uint8Array) => Promise<void>

Send a changeset to a peer via HTTP. Performs the 2-phase handshake first, then POST /mesh/changeset. Retries up to MAX_RETRIES times.

##### sendOnce()

(peerId: string, peerAddress: string, changeset: Uint8Array) => Promise<void>

##### close()

() => Promise<void>

Graceful shutdown.

#### SyncEngine

typeof SyncEngine

SyncEngine — periodic + event-driven peer-to-peer changeset exchange.  Security guarantees (per spec §5.1, §5.2, §10):  - Unsigned changesets are rejected before applyChanges.  - SHA-256 hash mismatch → changeset rejected + peer failure recorded.  - Corrupted Loro blobs detected by applyChanges + hash mismatch on inbound.  - Oversized changesets (10 MB) rejected.  - One peer failure does not block sync with other peers.

Members:

##### backend

Backend

##### transport

PeerTransport

##### discovery

PeerRegistry

##### identity

AgentIdentity

##### syncIntervalMs

number

##### maxPeerFailures

number

##### peerState

Map<string, PeerSyncState>

Per-peer sync state (agentId → PeerSyncState).

##### syncTimer

NodeJS.Timeout | undefined

##### running

boolean

##### inFlightSyncs

Set<Promise<void>>

##### dirty

boolean

Dirty flag: set when local backend is mutated; cleared after sync.

##### start()

() => Promise<void>

Start the sync engine:  1. Begin listening for inbound changesets via transport.  2. Start periodic sync loop.  3. Load persisted peer versions from backend (if supported).

##### stop()

() => Promise<void>

Stop the sync engine gracefully:  - Drain in-flight syncs.  - Shut down transport.

##### syncNow()

(peerId?: string) => Promise<void>

Trigger an immediate sync with all peers (or a specific peer).

##### _syncAllPeers()

() => Promise<void>

##### _syncPeer()

(peer: PeerInfo) => Promise<void>

##### _handleInbound()

(peerId: string, data: Uint8Array) => Promise<void>

##### _buildEnvelope()

(changesetBytes: Uint8Array, sinceVersion: bigint) => Promise<ChangesetEnvelope>

##### _verifyEnvelopeSignature()

(envelope: ChangesetEnvelope) => Promise<boolean>

##### _serializeEnvelope()

(envelope: ChangesetEnvelope) => Uint8Array

##### _deserializeEnvelope()

(data: Uint8Array) => ChangesetEnvelope

##### _getPeerState()

(agentId: string) => PeerSyncState

##### _recordPeerFailure()

(agentId: string, err: unknown) => void

##### _loadMeshState()

() => Promise<void>

Load lastSyncVersion per peer from the backend's llmtxt_mesh_state table. Falls back gracefully if the table/method does not exist.

##### _saveMeshState()

() => Promise<void>

##### getPeerStates()

() => ReadonlyMap<string, PeerSyncState>

Return current peer sync states for monitoring.

##### sha256Hex()

(data: Uint8Array) => string

SHA-256 of arbitrary bytes — exposed for testing integrity verification.

##### sha256Bytes()

(data: Uint8Array) => Uint8Array

#### MeshMessenger

typeof MeshMessenger

MeshMessenger — signed agent-to-agent routing over mesh transport.  Security guarantees (spec §7.1, §10):  - Outbound messages are Ed25519-signed before sending.  - Inbound messages are signature-verified against sender's known pubkey.  - Unsigned or invalid-signature messages are REJECTED silently (+ warn log).  - Payload 1 MB is rejected at send time.  - 1-hop relay for peers not directly connected.  - Local queue + retry on reconnect when no path exists.

Members:

##### identity

AgentIdentity

##### transport

PeerTransport

##### discovery

PeerRegistry

##### onMessage

((envelope: A2AEnvelope) => void) | undefined

##### knownPubkeys

Map<string, string>

agentId → pubkeyHex mapping built from discovered peers.

##### queue

Map<string, QueuedMessage[]>

Local queue for messages with no current path.

##### running

boolean

##### start()

() => Promise<void>

##### stop()

() => Promise<void>

##### send()

(to: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>) => Promise<void>

Send an A2A message to `to` agent.  Security: Signs the envelope with own Ed25519 key before sending. Payload must be JSON-serialisable and 1 MB.  Routing:  1. If `to` is in the current peer list → direct send.  2. Else → relay via any connected peer that knows `to` (1 hop).  3. If no path after MAX_RELAY_ATTEMPTS → queue locally.

##### retryQueued()

() => Promise<void>

Retry queued messages for all known peers. Call after discovery refresh or reconnect event.

##### _relay()

(envelope: A2AEnvelope, peers: PeerInfo[], attempt: number) => Promise<void>

##### _handleInbound()

(peerId: string, data: Uint8Array) => Promise<void>

##### _handleRelay()

(_fromPeerId: string, envelope: A2AEnvelope) => Promise<void>

##### _canonicalize()

(envelope: Omit<A2AEnvelope, "sig">) => Uint8Array

Build canonical JSON for signing (sorted keys, no whitespace).

##### _buildEnvelope()

(to: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>) => Promise<A2AEnvelope>

##### _verifySignature()

(envelope: A2AEnvelope) => Promise<boolean>

##### _enqueue()

(envelope: A2AEnvelope) => void

##### _flushQueue()

(targetId: string, peers: PeerInfo[]) => Promise<void>

##### _serialize()

(envelope: A2AEnvelope) => Uint8Array

##### _serializeRelay()

(frame: RelayFrame) => Uint8Array

##### _updateKnownPubkeys()

(peers: PeerInfo[]) => void

##### getQueueStatus()

() => Record<string, number>

Returns number of queued messages per target.

#### PostgresBackend

typeof PostgresBackend

PostgresBackend — Backend implementation using drizzle-orm/postgres-js.  Registers as `fastify.backendCore` via apps/backend/src/plugins/postgres-backend-plugin.ts. All route handlers call `fastify.backendCore.*` instead of querying Drizzle directly.

Example:
  import { PostgresBackend } from 'llmtxt/pg';
  
  const backend = new PostgresBackend({
    connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
  });
  await backend.open();
  const doc = await backend.createDocument({ title: 'My Doc', createdBy: 'agent-1' });
  await backend.close();

Members:

##### config

PostgresBackendConfig

##### _db

any

##### _sql

any

##### _isOpen

boolean

##### _s

SchemaCache

##### _orm

Record<string, any>

##### _appendDocumentEvent

AppendDocumentEventFn | null

##### _persistCrdtUpdate

PersistCrdtUpdateFn | null

##### _loadSectionState

LoadSectionStateFn | null

##### _subscribeCrdtUpdates

SubscribeCrdtUpdatesFn | null

##### _eventBus

DocumentEventBusLike | null

##### _crdtStateVector

CrdtStateVectorFn | null

##### _presenceRegistry

PresenceRegistryLike | null

##### _scratchpadPublish

ScratchpadPublishFn | null

##### _scratchpadRead

ScratchpadReadFn | null

##### _scratchpadSubscribe

ScratchpadSubscribeFn | null

##### _blobAdapter

any

##### open()

() => Promise<void>

Open the PostgreSQL connection pool. MUST be called before any other method. MUST be idempotent — calling open twice MUST NOT error.

##### setSchema()

(schema: SchemaCache) => void

##### setWaveBDeps()

(deps: { appendDocumentEvent: AppendDocumentEventFn; persistCrdtUpdate: PersistCrdtUpdateFn; loadSectionState: LoadSectionStateFn; subscribeCrdtUpdates: SubscribeCrdtUpdatesFn; eventBus: DocumentEventBusLike; crdtStateVector: CrdtStateVectorFn; }) => void

Inject Wave B event-log and CRDT dependencies. Called by postgres-backend-plugin.ts after open().  These dependencies live in apps/backend and cannot be statically imported from this package (monorepo boundary). Injecting them at plugin registration time keeps this class free of cross-package imports.

##### setBlobAdapter()

(adapter: any) => void

##### setWaveCDeps()

(deps: { presenceRegistry: PresenceRegistryLike; scratchpadPublish: ScratchpadPublishFn; scratchpadRead: ScratchpadReadFn; scratchpadSubscribe: ScratchpadSubscribeFn; }) => void

Inject Wave C presence + scratchpad dependencies. Called by postgres-backend-plugin.ts after open().  Presence is in-memory only (no PG persistence) — we delegate to the shared presenceRegistry singleton. Scratchpad uses Redis Streams with an in-process EventEmitter fallback.

##### close()

() => Promise<void>

Close the PostgreSQL connection pool. MUST stop all active connections. MUST be safe to call multiple times.

##### _assertOpen()

() => void

##### submitSignedApproval()

(params: { documentId: string; versionNumber: number; reviewerId: string; status: "APPROVED" | "REJECTED"; reason?: string; signatureBase64: string; }) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string; result?: ApprovalResult; }>

submitSignedApproval — Wave A-2 implementation.  Transactionally inserts an approval record, evaluates consensus, and auto-locks the document when consensus is reached. Appends approval.submitted / approval.rejected event if appendDocumentEvent has been injected.  params: documentId (slug), versionNumber, reviewerId, status, reason?,         signatureBase64 — plus optional idempotencyKey (cast via Record).

##### detachBlob()

(docSlug: string, blobName: string, detachedBy: string) => Promise<boolean>

##### fetchBlobByHash()

(hash: string) => Promise<Buffer | null>

##### exportDocument()

(params: ExportDocumentParams) => Promise<ExportDocumentResult>

Export a single document from Postgres to a file on disk.  Content retrieval:  1. Resolve slug → document row.  2. listVersions() to find the latest version.  3. getVersion() to get the full row (including compressedData).  4. Decompress compressedData → string content via the SDK decompress().

##### exportAll()

(params: ExportAllParams) => Promise<ExportAllResult>

Export all documents from the Postgres backend to a directory.

##### importDocument()

(params: ImportDocumentParams) => Promise<ImportDocumentResult>

Import a document from a file on disk into the Postgres backend.

##### getChangesSince()

(_dbVersion: bigint) => Promise<Uint8Array>

Not supported by PgBackend — cr-sqlite sync is a LocalBackend feature. PostgresBackend participates in P2P sync via a different protocol (P3).

##### applyChanges()

(_changeset: Uint8Array) => Promise<bigint>

#### PgContractAdapter

typeof PgContractAdapter

PgContractAdapter wraps PostgresBackend to satisfy the Backend interface expected by the contract test suite.  It caches title/createdBy per document id so assertions like `assert.equal(doc.title, 'Contract Test Doc')` pass even though the PG schema does not have a title column.

Members:

##### _inner

PostgresBackend

##### _meta

Map<string, { title: string; createdBy: string; }>

##### _publishCount

Map<string, number>

#### PeerRegistry

typeof PeerRegistry

PeerRegistry manages peer discovery for the P2P mesh.  ## Usage

Members:

##### meshDir

string

##### agentId

string

##### pubkeyB64

string

##### cleanupRegistered

boolean

##### register()

(info: PeerRegistration) => Promise<void>

Write this agent's `.peer` file to `$LLMTXT_MESH_DIR/<agentId>.peer`.  Also registers a `beforeExit` / `SIGTERM` handler to call `deregister()` on clean shutdown (P3 spec §3.2).

Parameters:
- info: Peer registration info. Defaults to the local agent's identity               if fields are omitted.

##### deregister()

() => Promise<void>

Delete this agent's `.peer` file (clean shutdown). No-op if the file does not exist.

##### discover()

() => Promise<PeerInfo[]>

Discover all peers by reading `*.peer` files from `$LLMTXT_MESH_DIR`.  Security: peer advertisements missing a valid `pubkey` or whose `pubkey` is inconsistent with their `agentId` are REJECTED and never returned. Stale peers (startedAt older than PEER_TTL_MS) are returned with `active: false`.

Returns: Array of validated `PeerInfo` (never includes the local agent).

##### loadStaticConfig()

(configPath?: string) => Promise<PeerInfo[]>

Load a static peer list from a JSON config file.  The file must contain an array of `PeerRegistration` objects. Entries missing a valid `pubkey` are rejected.

Parameters:
- configPath: Path to the config file.   Defaults to `~/.llmtxt/mesh.json`.

##### peerFilePath()

(agentId: string) => string

#### EventBus

typeof EventBus

In-process EventBus.  Implements `EventStream<unknown>` but callers typically use the typed helpers `publishTyped<T>` / `subscribeTyped<T>` for type safety.

Members:

##### _emitter

EventEmitter<[never]>

##### publish()

(channel: string, event: unknown) => void

Emit `event` on `channel`. All subscribers receive it synchronously.

##### subscribe()

(channel: string) => AsyncIterable<unknown>

Return an `AsyncIterable` that yields every event published on `channel` after the iterator is opened.

##### publishTyped()

<T>(channel: string, event: T) => void

Typed publish helper — no runtime cost, pure TS convenience.

##### subscribeTyped()

<T>(channel: string) => AsyncIterable<T>

Typed subscribe helper — no runtime cost, pure TS convenience.

#### ExternalBusAdapter

typeof ExternalBusAdapter

Adapts a `DocumentEventBusLike` (single `'document'` channel, slug-filtered) into an `EventSubscriber` that filters by `documentId` slug.  `ExternalBusAdapter` does NOT implement `EventPublisher` — publishing on the external bus is owned by the backend service layer (apps/backend), not by the SDK.  Usage in PostgresBackend:

Members:

##### subscribeBySlug()

<T extends { slug?: string; }>(slug: string) => AsyncIterable<T>

Subscribe to all `'document'` events and filter by `slug`.  Transforms the raw `BusDocumentEvent` shape into the SDK `DocumentEvent` shape.

##### subscribe()

(channel: string) => AsyncIterable<unknown>

#### PresenceManager

typeof PresenceManager

PresenceManager — in-memory, TTL-based presence state across mesh peers.  Thread-safety: Node.js is single-threaded; no locks required.  Security: Rate-limits inbound presence to max 1/peer/5s (spec §10, presence flood).

Members:

##### agentId

string

##### transport

PeerTransport

##### discovery

PeerRegistry

##### ttlSeconds

number

##### broadcastIntervalMs

number

##### rateLimitWindowMs

number

##### registry

Map<string, PresenceEntry>

Registry: agentId → PresenceEntry (includes self).

##### lastReceived

Map<string, number>

Rate-limit tracker: agentId → last-received epoch ms.

##### currentDocumentId

string | null

Own editable presence fields.

##### currentSectionId

string | null

##### broadcastTimer

NodeJS.Timeout | undefined

##### running

boolean

##### start()

() => Promise<void>

##### stop()

() => Promise<void>

##### setPresence()

(documentId: string | null, sectionId?: string | null) => void

Update own presence state (document/section being edited). Triggers an immediate broadcast.

##### getPresence()

(documentId?: string) => PresenceEntry[]

Returns all active (non-expired) agents currently in a document.

Parameters:
- documentId: Filter by document (or omit to get all active agents).

##### getAll()

() => PresenceEntry[]

Returns ALL active presence entries across all documents.

##### _handleInbound()

(peerId: string, data: Uint8Array) => void

Called by the transport listener for every incoming byte frame. Only processes messages with type byte 0x02 (presence).

##### _broadcastPresence()

() => Promise<void>

##### _serializePresence()

(entry: Omit<PresenceEntry, "receivedAt">) => Uint8Array

##### _isExpired()

(entry: PresenceEntry, nowMs: number) => boolean

##### _evictExpired()

() => void

#### AgentSessionError

typeof AgentSessionError

AgentSessionError: custom error for session lifecycle violations.  Code taxonomy: - SESSION_NOT_FOUND: No session found (e.g., during recovery) - SESSION_ALREADY_OPEN: open() called when not in Idle state - SESSION_NOT_ACTIVE: contribute() called when not in Active state - INVALID_STATE: Invalid state transition attempt - BACKEND_ERROR: Backend rejected the operation (wrapped original error) - SESSION_CLOSE_PARTIAL: close() completed with failures; see attached errors

Members:

##### code

string

##### cause

unknown

##### receipt

ContributionReceipt | undefined

Partial receipt attached when code is SESSION_CLOSE_PARTIAL.

##### errors

CloseStepError[] | undefined

Step-level errors attached when code is SESSION_CLOSE_PARTIAL.

#### AgentSession

typeof AgentSession

AgentSession: explicit, auditable lifecycle for ephemeral and persistent agents.  Usage:    const session = new AgentSession(     backend: remoteBackend,     agentId: 'agent-12345',   );    await session.open();   const result = await session.contribute((backend) =      return backend.createDocument( title: 'My Doc', createdBy: 'agent-12345' );   );   const receipt = await session.close();  State machine is mutex-protected to prevent concurrent close() calls.  Backend interface note (T461 follow-up):   The current Backend interface has no registerSession / unregisterSession /   flushPendingWrites / releaseAllLeases methods. open() uses joinPresence()   on a sentinel document ID derived from the sessionId to signal activity.   T461 will add dedicated session primitives to the Backend interface.  Receipt persistence note (T461 follow-up):   When documents were touched, close() calls backend.appendEvent() to persist   the receipt as a 'session.closed' event on the first touched document.   A dedicated backend.persistContributionReceipt() is deferred to T461.

Members:

##### state

AgentSessionState

##### sessionId

string

##### agentId

string

##### backend

Backend

##### label

string

##### openedAt

Date | undefined

##### closedAt

Date | undefined

##### cachedReceipt

ContributionReceipt | undefined

##### _documentIds

Set<string>

##### _eventCount

number

##### closeGuard

boolean

Mutex to protect close() from concurrent execution.

##### getState()

() => AgentSessionState

Get the current session state.

##### getSessionId()

() => string

Get the session ID.

##### getAgentId()

() => string

Get the agent ID.

##### getDocumentIds()

() => string[]

Get tracked document IDs.

##### getEventCount()

() => number

Get event count.

##### open()

() => Promise<void>

open(): Transition Idle - Open - Active.  Initialization steps (spec §3.2): 1. Guard: throw SESSION_ALREADY_OPEN if state !== Idle (not re-entrant) 2. Transition to Open 3. Record openedAt timestamp 4. Register presence via backend.joinPresence() (SHOULD per spec §3.2.4)    — uses a session sentinel doc ID since Backend has no registerSession().    T461 will add dedicated session primitives to Backend interface. 5. Transition to Active  Throws AgentSessionError: - SESSION_ALREADY_OPEN if state is not Idle - BACKEND_ERROR if backend rejects synchronously (async rejections are tolerated)

##### contribute()

<T>(fn: (backend: Backend) => Promise<T>) => Promise<T>

contribute(fn): Wrap and track a unit of work.  Requires state === Active. Wraps the caller's function and (spec §3.3): 1. Guard: throw SESSION_NOT_ACTIVE if state !== Active 2. Pass the session's backend instance to fn 3. On success:    a. Extract documentId / documentIds from the result (if object-shaped)    b. Increment eventCount 4. On error: propagate WITHOUT modifying eventCount or documentIds  Document ID tracking strategy:   The spec offers two options — proxy interception or caller-returned IDs.   We use the caller-returned approach: if fn returns an object with   `documentId` (string) or `documentIds` (string[]) fields, those are   extracted. This is zero-overhead and does not require Proxy.  Throws: - AgentSessionError(SESSION_NOT_ACTIVE) if state is not Active - Re-throws any error raised by fn (after leaving state as Active)

##### close()

() => Promise<ContributionReceipt>

close(): Transition Active - Closing - Closed.  Teardown steps (spec §3.4 — all attempted even if earlier steps fail): 1. Flush pending writes via backend.flushPendingWrites() if available 2. Drain A2A inbox: backend.pollA2AInbox(agentId) until empty 3. Release all leases (none tracked at session level — T461 will add    per-resource lease tracking; skipped with T461 note) 4. For LocalBackend: temp .db cleanup is deferred to T461 (backend owns paths) 5. Deregister presence: backend.leavePresence() 6. Build ContributionReceipt (documentIds sorted for determinism) 7. Persist receipt via backend.appendEvent() on first touched document 8. Return receipt  All teardown steps MUST be attempted even if earlier steps fail. Failures are collected and surfaced as SESSION_CLOSE_PARTIAL with the partial receipt and a list of CloseStepError. The receipt is always returned (or rethrown attached to the error).  Idempotency: calling close() on an already-closed session returns the cached receipt immediately without re-executing teardown steps.  Throws AgentSessionError: - INVALID_STATE if state is not Active or Closed (i.e., Idle, Open, Closing) - SESSION_CLOSE_PARTIAL if teardown completed with step failures  Note on leases: Per spec §3.4 step 3, leases should be released here. The current Backend interface tracks leases by resource key, not by session. AgentSession does not intercept acquireLease calls (it wraps via contribute()), so it cannot enumerate what the agent acquired. T461 will add a backend.releaseSessionLeases(sessionId) primitive. Until then, caller-acquired leases expire via TTL per the crash recovery contract (spec §5).

#### PostgresChangesetAdapter

typeof PostgresChangesetAdapter

PostgresChangesetAdapter — bridges PostgresBackend and the cr-sqlite mesh.  This adapter translates between:   - cr-sqlite binary changesets (used by LocalBackend for P2P sync), and   - Postgres row operations (used by PostgresBackend for persistence).  It is used by the optional POST /mesh/changeset route in apps/backend to allow api.llmtxt.my to participate as a mesh peer.  ## Usage (when fully implemented)

Members:

##### options

PostgresChangesetAdapterOptions

##### applyChangeset()

(_changesetBytes: Uint8Array) => Promise<number>

Apply a cr-sqlite binary changeset received from a LocalBackend mesh peer.  Steps (full implementation):   1. Deserialize the changeset using the cr-sqlite wire format.   2. For each row change, translate to a Postgres INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE.   3. Apply with LWW (Last-Write-Wins) conflict resolution using the      `crdt_state_hash` column for CRDT blob columns.   4. Return the new Postgres LSN/txid as the "applied db_version".

Parameters:
- changesetBytes: cr-sqlite binary changeset from a peer.

Returns: The Postgres txid after applying the changeset (used as sinceVersion).  TODO: Implement cr-sqlite → Postgres row translation.       See LocalBackend.applyChanges() for the cr-sqlite changeset format.

##### _applyRowChange()

(_change: PostgresRowChange) => Promise<void>

Apply a single row change to Postgres.  For INSERT/UPDATE: use ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE (upsert) for idempotency. For DELETE: soft-delete via a `deleted_at` timestamp if the schema supports it; hard-delete otherwise.  TODO: Implement per-table upsert logic.

##### getChangesSince()

(_sinceXid: number) => Promise<Uint8Array>

Serialize Postgres row changes since `sinceXid` into a cr-sqlite-compatible changeset binary for delivery to LocalBackend mesh peers.  Steps (full implementation):   1. Query all changed rows with txid  sinceXid from a Postgres audit/CDC table      (or use Postgres logical replication + pg_logical_emit_message).   2. Translate each Postgres row to a cr-sqlite changeset row entry.   3. Serialize to binary cr-sqlite changeset format.   4. Sign the changeset bytes (for SyncEngine signature verification).

Parameters:
- sinceXid: Postgres transaction ID of the last sync point.   Pass 0 for a full snapshot.

Returns: cr-sqlite binary changeset bytes, ready for SyncEngine.sendChangeset().  TODO: Implement Postgres row → cr-sqlite changeset translation.       Requires pg_stat_activity or logical decoding to capture row changes.

##### isReady()

() => boolean

Returns true if the adapter has been configured with a valid Postgres connection and is ready to accept inbound changesets.  In the stub implementation, always returns false (adapter not functional). Full implementation should ping the database.

#### A2AMessage

typeof A2AMessage

Signed A2A message envelope with builder API.  Construct via `buildA2AMessage` factory.

Members:

##### envelope

A2AEnvelope

##### verify()

(pubkeyHexOrBytes: string | Uint8Array) => Promise<boolean>

Verify this message's signature against a public key (32-byte hex or Uint8Array).

##### toJSON()

() => string

Serialize to JSON string.

### Interfaces

#### AwarenessState

AwarenessState

Presence state for a single agent.

Members:

##### agentId

string

##### section

string

##### cursorOffset

number | undefined

##### lastSeen

number

#### AwarenessEvent

AwarenessEvent

Members:

##### type

AwarenessEventType

##### clientId

number

##### state

AwarenessState | null

#### CacheStats

CacheStats

Snapshot of cache performance statistics.

Returned by `LRUCache.getStats`. The `hitRate` is calculated as `(hits / (hits + misses)) * 100` and rounded to two decimal places.

Members:

##### hits

number

Total number of cache hits since last reset.

##### misses

number

Total number of cache misses since last reset.

##### size

number

Current number of live (non-expired) entries in the cache.

##### maxSize

number

Maximum number of entries the cache can hold.

##### hitRate

number

Hit rate as a percentage (0-100), rounded to two decimal places.

#### LRUCacheOptions

LRUCacheOptions

Configuration options for constructing an `LRUCache` instance.

All fields are optional and have sensible defaults: 1000 entries max and a 24-hour TTL.

Members:

##### maxSize

number | undefined

Maximum number of entries before the least-recently-used entry is evicted (default: 1000).

##### ttl

number | undefined

Default time-to-live in milliseconds for new entries (default: 24 hours).

#### ClassificationResult

ClassificationResult

Result of `classifyContent`.  Mirrors `ClassificationResult` from `crates/llmtxt-core/src/classify/types.rs`.

Members:

##### mimeType

string

IANA MIME type, e.g. `"application/pdf"`, `"text/markdown"`, `"image/png"`.

##### category

"binary" | "text" | "structured" | "unknown"

Coarse content category.

##### format

"unknown" | "pdf" | "png" | "jpeg" | "gif" | "webp" | "avif" | "svg" | "mp4" | "webm" | "mp3" | "wav" | "ogg" | "zip" | "markdown" | "json" | "javascript" | "typescript" | "python" | "rust" | "go" | "plainText"

Specific content format.  Binary: `'pdf'` | `'png'` | `'jpeg'` | `'gif'` | `'webp'` | `'avif'` | `'svg'` |         `'mp4'` | `'webm'` | `'mp3'` | `'wav'` | `'ogg'` | `'zip'`  Text:   `'markdown'` | `'json'` | `'javascript'` | `'typescript'` | `'python'` |         `'rust'` | `'go'` | `'plainText'`  Fallback: `'unknown'`

##### confidence

number

Classification confidence in `[0.0, 1.0]`. See Rust spec for semantics.

##### isExtractable

boolean

Whether useful text content can be extracted from this format. `true` for text formats and PDF; `false` for images, audio, video, zip.

#### Section

Section

A logical section identified within a document.

Sections are detected by format-specific heuristics (headings in markdown, top-level keys in JSON, function/class declarations in code).

Members:

##### title

string

Display title of the section (heading text, JSON key, or symbol name).

##### depth

number

Nesting depth (0-based). Headings use depth = level - 1.

##### startLine

number

1-based line number where the section begins.

##### endLine

number

1-based line number where the section ends (inclusive).

##### tokenCount

number

Estimated token count for the section content.

##### type

"function" | "heading" | "json-key" | "code-block" | "class"

The structural type of the section.

#### DocumentOverview

DocumentOverview

High-level structural overview of a document.

Produced by `generateOverview`. Provides format detection, token counts, section listings, and format-specific extras (JSON keys or a markdown table of contents).

Members:

##### format

"text" | "markdown" | "json" | "code"

The detected document format.

##### lineCount

number

Total number of lines in the document.

##### tokenCount

number

Estimated total token count for the entire document.

##### sections

Section[]

Ordered list of sections found in the document.

##### keys

{ key: string; type: string; preview: string; }[] | undefined

Top-level JSON keys with type info and preview (JSON documents only).

##### toc

{ title: string; depth: number; line: number; }[] | undefined

Markdown table of contents entries (markdown documents only).

#### SearchResult

SearchResult

A single match returned by `searchContent`.

Members:

##### line

number

1-based line number of the matching line.

##### content

string

The full text of the matching line.

##### contextBefore

string[]

Lines immediately preceding the match (up to `contextLines`).

##### contextAfter

string[]

Lines immediately following the match (up to `contextLines`).

#### LineRangeResult

LineRangeResult

Result of extracting a line range from a document via `getLineRange`.

Members:

##### startLine

number

1-based line number where the extracted range begins.

##### endLine

number

1-based line number where the extracted range ends (inclusive).

##### content

string

The extracted text content for the requested line range.

##### tokenCount

number

Estimated token count for the extracted content.

##### totalLines

number

Total number of lines in the full document.

##### totalTokens

number

Estimated total token count for the full document.

##### tokensSaved

number

Number of tokens saved by extracting only this range.

#### DiffResult

DiffResult

Members:

##### addedLines

number

##### removedLines

number

##### addedTokens

number

##### removedTokens

number

#### StructuredDiffLine

StructuredDiffLine

A single line in a structured diff with type annotation and line numbers.

Members:

##### type

"context" | "added" | "removed"

##### content

string

##### oldLine

number | null

##### newLine

number | null

#### StructuredDiffResult

StructuredDiffResult

Full structured diff result with interleaved lines and summary counts.

Members:

##### lines

StructuredDiffLine[]

##### addedLineCount

number

##### removedLineCount

number

##### addedTokens

number

##### removedTokens

number

#### MultiDiffVariant

MultiDiffVariant

A single version variant at a divergent line position.

Members:

##### versionIndex

number

##### content

string

#### MultiDiffLine

MultiDiffLine

One line entry in a multi-way diff result.

Members:

##### lineNumber

number

##### type

"consensus" | "divergent" | "insertion"

"consensus" when all versions agree, "divergent" when versions differ,  "insertion" when a version adds a line not present in the base.

##### content

string

##### agreement

number

How many versions have `content` at this position.

##### total

number

Total number of versions (including the base).

##### variants

MultiDiffVariant[]

Per-version contents when type is "divergent"; empty for "consensus".

#### MultiDiffStats

MultiDiffStats

Aggregate statistics for a multi-way diff.

Members:

##### totalLines

number

##### consensusLines

number

##### divergentLines

number

##### consensusPercentage

number

#### MultiDiffResult

MultiDiffResult

Full result of a multi-way diff.

Members:

##### baseVersion

number

##### versionCount

number

##### lines

MultiDiffLine[]

##### stats

MultiDiffStats

#### CherryPickProvenance

CherryPickProvenance

A single provenance entry in the cherry-pick merged output.

Members:

##### lineStart

number

##### lineEnd

number

##### fromVersion

number

##### fillFrom

boolean | undefined

#### CherryPickStats

CherryPickStats

Statistics for a cherry-pick merge operation.

Members:

##### totalLines

number

##### sourcesUsed

number

##### sectionsExtracted

number

##### lineRangesExtracted

number

#### CherryPickResult

CherryPickResult

Return value of a cherry-pick merge operation.

Members:

##### content

string

##### provenance

CherryPickProvenance[]

##### stats

CherryPickStats

#### Conflict

Conflict

A single conflict region from a 3-way merge.

Members:

##### oursStart

number

1-based start line of the conflicting region in `ours`.

##### oursEnd

number

1-based end line of the conflicting region in `ours` (inclusive).

##### theirsStart

number

1-based start line of the conflicting region in `theirs`.

##### theirsEnd

number

1-based end line of the conflicting region in `theirs` (inclusive).

##### baseStart

number

1-based start line of the conflicting region in the common ancestor.

##### baseEnd

number

1-based end line of the conflicting region in the common ancestor.

##### oursContent

string

The conflicting text from `ours`.

##### theirsContent

string

The conflicting text from `theirs`.

##### baseContent

string

The original text from the common ancestor.

#### MergeStats

MergeStats

Statistics for a 3-way merge operation.

Members:

##### totalLines

number

Total lines in the merged output (including conflict markers).

##### autoMergedLines

number

Number of lines accepted without conflict.

##### conflictCount

number

Number of distinct conflict regions.

#### ThreeWayMergeResult

ThreeWayMergeResult

Full result of a 3-way merge operation.

Members:

##### merged

string

The merged document content, with conflict markers where applicable.

##### hasConflicts

boolean

`true` when at least one conflict could not be auto-merged.

##### conflicts

Conflict[]

Details of each conflict region.

##### stats

MergeStats

Summary statistics for the merge.

#### SectionSimilarity

SectionSimilarity

Per-section similarity record produced by semantic diff.

Members:

##### sectionA

string

##### sectionB

string

##### similarity

number

##### alignment

SectionAlignment

#### SemanticChange

SemanticChange

A semantic change annotation for a matched/renamed section pair.

Members:

##### changeType

string

One of: "unchanged", "rephrased", "modified", "rewritten".

##### section

string

##### similarity

number

##### description

string

#### SemanticDiffResult

SemanticDiffResult

Full result of a semantic diff between two document versions.

Members:

##### overallSimilarity

number

##### sectionSimilarities

SectionSimilarity[]

##### semanticChanges

SemanticChange[]

#### ReviewCluster

ReviewCluster

A cluster of reviewers whose embeddings are mutually similar.

Members:

##### members

string[]

##### avgSimilarity

number

#### SemanticConsensusResult

SemanticConsensusResult

Result of semantic consensus evaluation across a set of reviews.

Members:

##### consensus

boolean

##### agreementScore

number

##### clusters

ReviewCluster[]

##### outliers

string[]

#### GraphNode

GraphNode

A node in the knowledge graph.

Members:

##### id

string

##### type

string

##### label

string

##### weight

number

#### GraphEdge

GraphEdge

An edge in the knowledge graph.

Members:

##### source

string

##### target

string

##### type

string

##### weight

number

#### GraphStats

GraphStats

Statistics for a knowledge graph.

Members:

##### agentCount

number

##### topicCount

number

##### decisionCount

number

##### edgeCount

number

#### KnowledgeGraph

KnowledgeGraph

A knowledge graph containing nodes, edges, and statistics.

Members:

##### nodes

GraphNode[]

##### edges

GraphEdge[]

##### stats

GraphStats

#### MessageInput

MessageInput

Input message for graph construction.

Members:

##### id

string

##### fromAgentId

string

##### content

string

##### metadata

{ mentions?: string[]; directives?: string[]; tags?: string[]; } | undefined

##### createdAt

string

#### SimilarityRankResult

SimilarityRankResult

Result entry from rankBySimilarity.

Members:

##### index

number

##### score

number

#### ApiVersionInfo

ApiVersionInfo

Metadata for a specific API version.

Members:

##### version

number

##### deprecated

boolean

##### sunset

string | undefined

ISO 8601 date on which this version will stop being served.

#### DocumentMeta

DocumentMeta

Metadata for a stored document.

Members:

##### id

string

##### slug

string

##### format

ContentFormat

##### contentHash

string

##### originalSize

number

##### compressedSize

number

##### tokenCount

number

##### createdAt

number

##### expiresAt

number | null

##### accessCount

number

##### lastAccessedAt

number | null

##### mode

DocumentMode | undefined

Lifecycle state (collaborative documents).

##### versionCount

number | undefined

Total number of versions.

##### currentVersion

number | undefined

Current version number.

##### storageKey

string | undefined

Object storage key when content lives in S3 instead of inline.

#### VersionMeta

VersionMeta

Metadata for a single document version.

Members:

##### id

string

##### documentId

string

##### versionNumber

number

##### contentHash

string

##### tokenCount

number

##### createdAt

number

##### createdBy

string | undefined

##### changelog

string | undefined

#### VersionSummary

VersionSummary

Summary of a version for listing (no content).

Members:

##### versionNumber

number

##### tokenCount

number

##### createdAt

number

##### createdBy

string | undefined

##### changelog

string | undefined

#### VersionDiff

VersionDiff

Result of comparing two versions.

Members:

##### documentId

string

##### fromVersion

number

##### toVersion

number

##### addedTokens

number

##### removedTokens

number

##### addedLines

number

##### removedLines

number

#### LlmtxtRef

LlmtxtRef

Reference to an llmtxt document shared in a message.

Members:

##### slug

string

##### url

string

##### format

ContentFormat

##### tokenCount

number

##### preview

string

#### AttachmentOptions

AttachmentOptions

Options for creating an attachment via the bridge.

Members:

##### content

string

##### format

ContentFormat | undefined

##### conversationId

string

##### fromAgentId

string

##### expiresInMs

number | undefined

#### AttachmentReshareOptions

AttachmentReshareOptions

Options for re-sharing an existing attachment.

Members:

##### expiresIn

number | undefined

##### mode

AttachmentSharingMode | undefined

#### AttachmentVersionOptions

AttachmentVersionOptions

Options for appending a version to an existing attachment slug.

Members:

##### baseVersion

number | undefined

##### changelog

string | undefined

#### DocumentEvent

DocumentEvent

Payload for a document lifecycle event.  Emitted by the in-process event bus after a successful database write. Consumers include WebSocket/SSE streams and webhook delivery workers.

Members:

##### type

DocumentEventType

Discriminant — consumers can switch on this.

##### slug

string

Short URL slug of the affected document.

##### documentId

string

Opaque document primary key.

##### timestamp

number

Unix timestamp in milliseconds.

##### actor

string

userId or agentId that triggered the event. 'system' for auto-actions.

##### data

Record<string, unknown>

Event-specific supplemental data.

#### LlmtxtClientConfig

LlmtxtClientConfig

Members:

##### apiBase

string

##### apiKey

string

##### agentId

string

#### UploadResult

UploadResult

Members:

##### slug

string

##### contentHash

string

##### originalSize

number

##### compressedSize

number

##### compressionRatio

number

##### tokens

number

##### signedUrl

string

##### expiresAt

number

#### FetchResult

FetchResult

Members:

##### slug

string

##### content

string

##### format

string

##### title

string | undefined

##### contentHash

string

##### originalSize

number

##### tokens

number

#### ReshareResult

ReshareResult

Members:

##### slug

string

##### mode

AttachmentSharingMode

##### signedUrl

string | undefined

##### expiresAt

number | null | undefined

#### AttachmentVersionResult

AttachmentVersionResult

Members:

##### slug

string

##### versionNumber

number

##### patchText

string | undefined

##### contentHash

string | undefined

##### createdAt

string | undefined

##### createdBy

string | undefined

##### changelog

string | undefined

#### SectionDelta

SectionDelta

A delta event emitted when the CRDT state for a section changes.

Members:

##### slug

string

Document slug.

##### sectionId

string

Section identifier.

##### text

string

Current plain-text content of the section after applying the delta.

##### updateBytes

Uint8Array<ArrayBufferLike>

Raw Loro update bytes that caused this delta (Uint8Array). These are Loro binary format bytes — bitwise incompatible with the previous Yrs lib0 v1 format.

##### receivedAt

number

Wall clock timestamp (ms since epoch) when the update was received.

#### SubscribeSectionOptions

SubscribeSectionOptions

Options for `subscribeSection`.

Members:

##### baseUrl

string | undefined

Base URL of the llmtxt API. Defaults to `https://api.llmtxt.my`.

##### token

string | undefined

Bearer token for authentication. Pass your API key here (llmtxt_... format).

##### onError

((err: Event) => void) | undefined

Called when the WebSocket encounters an error.

##### onAwareness

((payload: Uint8Array) => void) | undefined

Called when an AwarenessRelay (0x04) message is received. The payload is the raw awareness bytes relayed from a peer.

#### EmbeddingProvider

EmbeddingProvider

Standard embedding provider interface — matches apps/backend/src/utils/embeddings.ts. Implemented by both the local ONNX provider and the TF-IDF fallback.

Members:

##### embed()

(texts: string[]) => Promise<number[][]>

##### dimensions

number

##### model

string

##### provider

string

#### SignatureHeaders

SignatureHeaders

HTTP headers produced by `AgentIdentity.buildSignatureHeaders` / `signRequest`.

Members:

##### 'X-Agent-Pubkey-Id'

string

Agent identifier used to look up the registered public key on the server.

##### 'X-Agent-Signature'

string

Hex-encoded 64-byte Ed25519 signature over the canonical payload.

##### 'X-Agent-Nonce'

string

Hex-encoded random nonce (16 bytes = 32 hex chars).

##### 'X-Agent-Timestamp'

string

Milliseconds since epoch as a decimal string.

#### CanonicalPayloadOptions

CanonicalPayloadOptions

Options for `buildCanonicalPayload`.  All fields map directly to the Rust `canonical_payload()` parameters in `crates/llmtxt-core/src/identity.rs`.

Members:

##### method

string

HTTP method, uppercase (e.g. "PUT").

##### path

string

Path and query string (e.g. "/api/v1/documents/abc").

##### timestampMs

number

Milliseconds since epoch.

##### agentId

string

Agent identifier registered on the server.

##### nonceHex

string

Hex-encoded nonce (≥ 16 bytes = ≥ 32 hex chars).

##### bodyHashHex

string

Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the request body bytes (64 chars).

#### ValidationResult

ValidationResult<T>

Outcome of a content validation operation.

Returned by every validation function in this module. When `success` is `true`, `data` holds the parsed value and `format` indicates the detected content type. When `success` is `false`, `errors` describes what went wrong.

Members:

##### success

boolean

Whether the validation passed without errors.

##### data

T | undefined

The parsed/validated data, present only when `success` is `true`.

##### errors

ValidationError[] | undefined

List of validation errors, present only when `success` is `false`.

##### format

"text" | "markdown" | "json" | undefined

The detected or requested content format, when determinable.

#### ValidationError

ValidationError

A single validation error with location, message, and error code.

Modeled after Zod issue objects but flattened for easy serialization. The `path` uses dot-delimited notation (empty string for the root value).

Members:

##### path

string

Dot-delimited path to the field that failed validation (empty string for root).

##### message

string

Human-readable description of the validation failure.

##### code

string

Machine-readable error code (e.g. `"invalid_json"`, `"unknown_schema"`).

#### ValidateContentOptions

ValidateContentOptions

Options for content validation.

Members:

##### schemaName

string | undefined

Optional predefined schema name for JSON validation.

##### maxBytes

number | undefined

Maximum content size in bytes. Set to 0 to disable. Default: 5 MB.

##### maxLineBytes

number | undefined

Maximum line length in bytes. Set to 0 to disable. Default: 64 KB.

##### rejectBinary

boolean | undefined

Reject content with binary control characters (0x00-0x08). Default: true.

#### SignedUrlParams

SignedUrlParams

Parameters that uniquely identify a signed URL access grant.

Members:

##### slug

string

##### agentId

string

##### conversationId

string

##### expiresAt

number

#### SignedUrlConfig

SignedUrlConfig

Configuration for generating and verifying signed URLs.

Members:

##### secret

string

##### baseUrl

string

##### pathPrefix

string | undefined

Optional path prefix like `/attachments`. Default: root path.

##### signatureLength

number | undefined

Signature length in hex chars. Default: 16.

#### VerifyResult

VerifyResult

Outcome of verifying a signed URL.

Members:

##### valid

boolean

##### reason

"missing_params" | "expired" | "invalid_signature" | undefined

##### params

SignedUrlParams | undefined

#### OrgSignedUrlParams

OrgSignedUrlParams

Parameters for org-scoped signed URLs (Phase 5 enterprise). Extends conversation-scoped params with an organization ID.

Members:

##### orgId

string

#### SnapshotMeta

SnapshotMeta

Members:

##### sessionId

string

##### agentId

string

##### createdAt

string

##### originalSize

number

##### compressedSize

number

##### compressionRatio

number

##### tokens

number

##### contentHash

string

#### CompressedSnapshot

CompressedSnapshot

Members:

##### meta

SnapshotMeta

##### data

Buffer<ArrayBufferLike>

#### SnapshotOptions

SnapshotOptions

Members:

##### includeDecisions

boolean | undefined

Include full decision log (default: true)

##### includeObservations

boolean | undefined

Include brain observations (default: true)

##### maxSize

number | undefined

Max content size before compression in bytes (default: 5MB)

#### DocumentEventLogEntry

DocumentEventLogEntry

A single event row from the document event log.

Members:

##### id

string

##### seq

string

Monotonically increasing per-document sequence number (as string — bigint-safe).

##### event_type

string

##### actor_id

string

##### payload

Record<string, unknown>

##### created_at

string

#### WatchDocumentOptions

WatchDocumentOptions

Members:

##### fromSeq

string | number | undefined

Resume from this sequence number (exclusive). If unset, starts from the beginning.

##### apiKey

string | undefined

Bearer token or API key for authenticated requests. Passed as `Authorization: Bearer <apiKey>` header.

##### maxReconnects

number | undefined

Maximum reconnect attempts within the circuit-breaker window (60s). Defaults to 5.

##### signal

AbortSignal | undefined

AbortSignal to cancel the stream externally.

#### StateTransition

StateTransition

Record of a lifecycle state change.

Members:

##### from

DocumentState

State before the transition.

##### to

DocumentState

State after the transition.

##### changedBy

string

Agent that initiated the transition.

##### changedAt

number

Timestamp of the transition (ms since epoch).

##### reason

string | undefined

Human-readable reason for the transition.

##### atVersion

number

Document version number at the time of transition.

#### TransitionResult

TransitionResult

Result of attempting a state transition.

Members:

##### valid

boolean

Whether the transition is allowed.

##### reason

string | undefined

Human-readable explanation when invalid.

##### allowedTargets

readonly DocumentState[]

The allowed targets from the current state (for error context).

#### VersionEntry

VersionEntry

A single version entry in a document's patch stack.

Members:

##### versionNumber

number

Sequential version number (1-based).

##### patchText

string

Unified diff patch text.

##### createdBy

string

Agent that authored this version.

##### changelog

string

One-line description of the change.

##### contentHash

string

SHA-256 hash of the resulting content after applying this patch.

##### createdAt

number

Timestamp of creation (ms since epoch).

#### ReconstructionResult

ReconstructionResult

Result of reconstructing a document at a specific version.

Members:

##### content

string

The document content at the requested version.

##### version

number

The version number that was reconstructed.

##### patchesApplied

number

Number of patches applied to reach this version.

##### contentHash

string

SHA-256 hash of the reconstructed content.

##### tokenCount

number

Token count of the reconstructed content.

#### PatchValidationResult

PatchValidationResult

Result of validating whether a patch applies cleanly.

Members:

##### applies

boolean

Whether the patch can be applied without conflicts.

##### error

string | undefined

Error message if the patch does not apply.

##### resultContent

string | undefined

The content that would result if the patch applies.

#### VersionDiffSummary

VersionDiffSummary

Summary of changes between two versions.

Members:

##### fromVersion

number

Source version number.

##### toVersion

number

Target version number.

##### addedLines

number

Lines added between versions.

##### removedLines

number

Lines removed between versions.

##### addedTokens

number

Tokens added between versions.

##### removedTokens

number

Tokens removed between versions.

##### patchText

string

Unified diff text.

#### Review

Review

A single review from an agent.

Members:

##### reviewerId

string

Agent that submitted the review.

##### status

ApprovalStatus

Current status of this review.

##### timestamp

number

Timestamp of the review action (ms since epoch).

##### reason

string | undefined

Reason or comment provided with the review.

##### atVersion

number

Version number the review applies to (`STALE` if document changed since).

#### ApprovalPolicy

ApprovalPolicy

Policy governing how approvals are evaluated.

Members:

##### requiredCount

number

Minimum number of approvals required (absolute count).  Ignored when `requiredPercentage` is set ( 0).

##### requireUnanimous

boolean

If true, all allowed reviewers must approve (overrides count/percentage).

##### allowedReviewerIds

string[]

Agent IDs allowed to review. Empty means anyone can review.

##### timeoutMs

number

Auto-expire reviews older than this (ms). 0 means no timeout.

##### requiredPercentage

number | undefined

Percentage of effective reviewers required (0-100). 0 means use requiredCount.  When  0, threshold = ceil(percentage * effectiveReviewerCount / 100).

#### ApprovalResult

ApprovalResult

Result of evaluating reviews against a policy.

Members:

##### approved

boolean

Whether the approval threshold is met.

##### approvedBy

string[]

Reviewers that have approved.

##### rejectedBy

string[]

Reviewers that have rejected.

##### pendingFrom

string[]

Reviewers that are still pending.

##### staleFrom

string[]

Reviewers whose reviews are stale (document changed since).

##### reason

string

Human-readable summary of the evaluation.

#### ContentRef

ContentRef

Reference to where a document's compressed content lives.  Inline: content is stored directly in the database (small documents). Object-store: content is stored in S3-compatible storage, referenced by key.

Members:

##### type

StorageType

Storage backend type.

##### storageKey

string | undefined

Location of the content. - For `inline`: not used (content is in the database row). - For `object-store`: the object key (e.g. `attachments/xK9mP2nQ/v3.zlib`).

##### contentHash

string

SHA-256 hash of the uncompressed content for integrity verification.

##### originalSize

number

Size of the uncompressed content in bytes.

##### compressedSize

number

Size of the compressed content in bytes.

##### compression

CompressionMethod

Compression method used.

#### StorageMetadata

StorageMetadata

Metadata about a stored document blob.

Members:

##### ref

ContentRef

Content reference.

##### createdAt

number

When the content was first stored (ms since epoch).

##### lastAccessedAt

number

When the content was last accessed (ms since epoch).

##### accessCount

number

Number of times the content has been accessed.

#### StorageAdapter

StorageAdapter

Storage adapter that platforms implement.

Members:

##### getContent()

(slug: string, version?: number) => Promise<string>

Retrieve document content at a specific version. When version is omitted, returns the latest version's content.

##### putContent()

(slug: string, version: number, content: string) => Promise<ContentRef>

Store document content for a specific version. Returns a ContentRef indicating where the content was stored.

##### getVersions()

(slug: string) => Promise<VersionEntry[]>

Get the ordered list of version entries for a document.

##### addVersion()

(slug: string, entry: VersionEntry) => Promise<void>

Append a new version entry to the document's version stack.

##### getState()

(slug: string) => Promise<DocumentState>

Get the current lifecycle state of a document.

##### setState()

(slug: string, transition: StateTransition) => Promise<void>

Record a lifecycle state transition.

##### getReviews()

(slug: string) => Promise<Review[]>

Get all reviews for a document.

##### addReview()

(slug: string, review: Review) => Promise<void>

Add or update a review for a document.

##### getApprovalPolicy()

(slug: string) => Promise<ApprovalPolicy>

Get the approval policy for a document.

#### VersionAttribution

VersionAttribution

Attribution data for a single version.

Members:

##### versionNumber

number

Version number this attribution describes.

##### authorId

string

Agent that authored the change.

##### addedLines

number

Lines added in this version.

##### removedLines

number

Lines removed in this version.

##### addedTokens

number

Tokens added in this version.

##### removedTokens

number

Tokens removed in this version.

##### sectionsModified

string[]

Section titles that were modified (detected via structural analysis).

##### changelog

string

One-line change description.

##### createdAt

number

Timestamp of the change.

#### ContributorSummary

ContributorSummary

Aggregated contribution summary for a single agent.

Members:

##### agentId

string

Agent identifier.

##### versionsAuthored

number

Number of versions this agent authored.

##### totalTokensAdded

number

Total tokens added across all versions.

##### totalTokensRemoved

number

Total tokens removed across all versions.

##### netTokens

number

Net token impact (added - removed).

##### firstContribution

number

Timestamp of first contribution.

##### lastContribution

number

Timestamp of most recent contribution.

##### sectionsModified

string[]

Unique section titles this agent modified.

#### PlannedSection

PlannedSection

A section selected for retrieval.

Members:

##### sectionIndex

number

Index into the overview's sections array.

##### title

string

Section title.

##### tokenCount

number

Estimated token cost for this section.

##### reason

"query-match" | "structural" | "budget-fit"

Why this section was selected.

##### score

number | undefined

Relevance score (0-1) when selected by query match.

#### RetrievalPlan

RetrievalPlan

The output of retrieval planning.

Members:

##### sections

PlannedSection[]

Sections selected for retrieval, ordered by relevance.

##### totalTokens

number

Total tokens across all selected sections.

##### budgetRemaining

number

Remaining token budget after selection.

##### tokensSaved

number

Tokens saved compared to fetching the full document.

##### fullDocumentFits

boolean

Whether the full document fits within budget (no planning needed).

#### RetrievalOptions

RetrievalOptions

Options for retrieval planning.

Members:

##### minScore

number | undefined

Minimum similarity score to include a section (0-1). Default: 0.1

##### includeIntro

boolean | undefined

Always include the first section (typically title/intro). Default: true

#### CreateVersionOptions

CreateVersionOptions

Options for creating a new version.

Members:

##### agentId

string

Agent creating this version.

##### changelog

string

One-line change description.

#### LlmtxtDocumentOptions

LlmtxtDocumentOptions

Options for constructing an LlmtxtDocument.

Members:

##### slug

string

Document slug.

##### storage

StorageAdapter

Storage adapter for persistence.

#### Lease

Lease

Members:

##### leaseId

string

##### holder

string

##### expiresAt

string

#### LeaseOptions

LeaseOptions

Members:

##### baseUrl

string

##### apiKey

string

#### SectionDelta

SectionDelta

Section-level delta returned by fetchSectionDelta and included in diff-mode SSE payloads.

Members:

##### added

string[]

##### modified

{ name: string; content: string; }[]

##### deleted

string[]

##### fromSeq

number

##### toSeq

number

#### SectionDeltaResponse

SectionDeltaResponse

Members:

##### delta

SectionDelta | null

##### currentSeq

number

#### SubscribeOptions

SubscribeOptions

Members:

##### since

number | undefined

Resume from a specific sequence number (sent as Last-Event-ID).

##### mode

"events" | "diff" | undefined

'events' = raw event payloads; 'diff' = payloads include computed content diffs.

##### baseUrl

string

Base URL of the API (no trailing slash).

##### apiKey

string

Bearer API key.

#### SubscriptionEvent

SubscriptionEvent

Members:

##### seq

number

Monotonic sequence number (from Last-Event-ID).

##### type

string

Event type string (e.g. 'version.published', 'SECTION_LEASED').

##### path

string

The path pattern the subscription was opened with.

##### payload

unknown

Raw event payload.

##### delta

SectionDelta | undefined

Section-level delta, present when mode='diff' and the server computed one.

#### StandaloneConfig

StandaloneConfig

Config for standalone topology.  One agent, one local `.db` file, zero network dependency. Use for single developer or single agent, offline-first operation, local testing.

Members:

##### topology

"standalone"

##### storagePath

string | undefined

Path for the local .db file. Defaults to '.llmtxt'.

##### identityPath

string | undefined

Optional path to agent identity keypair.

##### crsqlite

boolean | undefined

Set true to enable cr-sqlite (T385). Default: false.

##### crsqliteExtPath

string | undefined

Path to crsqlite extension (optional, see P2-cr-sqlite.md).

#### HubSpokeConfig

HubSpokeConfig

Config for hub-and-spoke topology.  One hub (PostgresBackend or a designated LocalBackend) is the Single Source of Truth. N spokes are RemoteBackend clients that write to and read from the hub. Ephemeral swarm workers are spokes with no local `.db` file.

Members:

##### topology

"hub-spoke"

##### hubUrl

string

URL of the hub API instance (e.g. 'https://api.llmtxt.my'). REQUIRED — validation MUST fail fast if absent.

##### apiKey

string | undefined

API key for authenticating with the hub. MUST be present for write operations.

##### identityPath

string | undefined

Ed25519 private key hex for signing writes (alternative to apiKey). If both are supplied, Ed25519 signed writes take precedence.

##### persistLocally

boolean | undefined

When true, this spoke maintains a local cr-sqlite replica. Requires T385 (cr-sqlite) to be installed. Default: false (ephemeral swarm worker mode — no .db file).

##### storagePath

string | undefined

Required when persistLocally=true. Path to local .db file.

#### MeshConfig

MeshConfig

Config for mesh topology.  N persistent peers, each with their own cr-sqlite LocalBackend. No central hub is required. Peers sync directly with each other via the P2P transport defined in T386. Use for offline-first P2P collaboration, air-gapped environments, or small teams of persistent agents (≤10 peers).

Members:

##### topology

"mesh"

##### storagePath

string

Path for the local cr-sqlite .db file. REQUIRED for mesh.

##### identityPath

string | undefined

Optional path to agent identity keypair. Defaults to storagePath/identity.json.

##### peers

string[] | undefined

Known peers at startup. Each entry is a transport address. Format: 'unix:/path/to/sock' | 'http://host:port'

##### meshDir

string | undefined

Directory where peer advertisement files are written and read. Defaults to $LLMTXT_MESH_DIR or '/tmp/llmtxt-mesh'.

##### transport

"unix" | "http" | undefined

Transport to listen on. Default: 'unix'.

##### port

number | undefined

Port for http transport. Default: 7642.

#### BackendConfig

BackendConfig

Configuration for a Backend instance.  BackendConfig is passed to the backend constructor. LocalBackend uses storagePath + identityPath; RemoteBackend uses baseUrl + apiKey.

Members:

##### storagePath

string | undefined

Directory where the backend stores its data. For LocalBackend: SQLite DB and large content blobs live here. Defaults to '.llmtxt' relative to the working directory.

##### identityPath

string | undefined

Path to the agent identity keypair JSON file. Defaults to /identity.json.

##### baseUrl

string | undefined

Base URL of a remote LLMtxt API instance. Required for RemoteBackend. MUST include scheme (https://).

##### apiKey

string | undefined

API key for authenticating with the remote instance. Used by RemoteBackend in the Authorization header.

##### wal

boolean | undefined

SQLite WAL mode. Defaults to true. Only relevant for LocalBackend.

##### leaseReaperIntervalMs

number | undefined

Lease reaper interval in milliseconds. Defaults to 10_000. Only relevant for LocalBackend.

##### presenceTtlMs

number | undefined

Presence TTL in milliseconds. Defaults to 30_000. Only relevant for LocalBackend.

##### crsqliteExtPath

string | undefined

Absolute path to a pre-downloaded crsqlite native extension (.so / .dylib / .dll). When supplied, LocalBackend uses this path instead of resolving the extension via vlcn.io/crsqlite. Useful in air-gapped or bundled environments where the install-time binary download is not possible.  DR-P2-01: If absent, LocalBackend attempts to resolve the path from the vlcn.io/crsqlite optional peer dependency. If neither is available, LocalBackend opens without cr-sqlite (hasCRR = false, no crash).

##### maxBlobSizeBytes

number | undefined

Maximum blob size in bytes. Defaults to 100 * 1024 * 1024 (100 MB).

##### blobStorageMode

"s3" | "pg-lo" | undefined

Blob storage mode for PostgresBackend. 's3' uses S3/R2 object storage (default). 'pg-lo' uses PostgreSQL large objects.

##### s3Endpoint

string | undefined

S3/R2 endpoint URL (e.g. "https://s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com").

##### s3Bucket

string | undefined

S3/R2 bucket name. Required when blobStorageMode = 's3'.

##### s3Region

string | undefined

S3/R2 region (e.g. "us-east-1").

##### s3AccessKeyId

string | undefined

S3/R2 access key ID.

##### s3SecretAccessKey

string | undefined

S3/R2 secret access key.

#### Document

Document

A stored document record.

Members:

##### id

string

Unique document identifier (nanoid).

##### slug

string

URL-safe slug derived from the title. Unique per backend.

##### title

string

Human-readable document title.

##### state

DocumentState

Current lifecycle state.

##### createdBy

string

Agent that created the document.

##### createdAt

number

Creation timestamp (ms since epoch).

##### updatedAt

number

Last modified timestamp (ms since epoch).

##### versionCount

number

Current version count.

##### labels

string[] | undefined

Arbitrary metadata labels.

#### CreateDocumentParams

CreateDocumentParams

Parameters for creating a document.

Members:

##### title

string

##### createdBy

string

##### labels

string[] | undefined

##### slug

string | undefined

If supplied, slug is used as-is instead of being derived from title.

#### ListDocumentsParams

ListDocumentsParams

Parameters for listing documents.

Members:

##### cursor

string | undefined

Cursor for pagination (document id).

##### limit

number | undefined

Maximum number of results. Defaults to 20.

##### state

DocumentState | undefined

Filter by state.

##### createdBy

string | undefined

Filter by creator.

#### ListResult

ListResult<T>

Paginated list result.

Members:

##### items

T[]

##### nextCursor

string | null

Cursor to pass for the next page, or null if no more results.

#### PublishVersionParams

PublishVersionParams

Parameters for publishing a new version.

Members:

##### documentId

string

##### content

string

Full content of this version (before patching).

##### patchText

string

Unified diff patch text from previous version. Empty string for v1.

##### createdBy

string

Agent creating this version.

##### changelog

string

One-line description of the change.

#### TransitionParams

TransitionParams

Parameters for transitioning a document's lifecycle state.

Members:

##### documentId

string

##### to

DocumentState

##### changedBy

string

##### reason

string | undefined

#### DocumentEvent

Members:

##### type

##### agentId

##### payload

#### AppendEventParams

string

##### agentId

string

##### payload

#### QueryEventsParams

QueryEventsParams

Parameters for querying events.

Members:

##### documentId

string

##### type

string | undefined

Filter by event type.

##### since

string | undefined

Return events after this cursor (event id).

##### limit

number | undefined

Maximum results. Defaults to 50.

#### CrdtUpdate

CrdtUpdate

A CRDT update payload for a document section.

Members:

##### documentId

##### sectionKey

string

##### updateBase64

string

Yjs binary update encoded as base64.

##### agentId

string

##### createdAt

number

#### CrdtState

CrdtState

Current CRDT state for a section.

Members:

##### documentId

string

##### sectionKey

string

##### stateVectorBase64

string

Serialized Yjs state vector as base64.

##### snapshotBase64

string

Current merged document state as base64.

##### updatedAt

number

#### Lease

Lease

A distributed lock / lease record.

Members:

##### id

string

##### resource

string

Resource being locked (e.g. 'document:abc123').

##### holder

string

Agent holding the lease.

##### expiresAt

number

Expiry timestamp (ms since epoch). 0 = never expires.

##### acquiredAt

number

#### AcquireLeaseParams

AcquireLeaseParams

Parameters for acquiring a lease.

Members:

##### resource

string

##### holder

string

##### ttlMs

number

Lease TTL in milliseconds.

#### PresenceEntry

PresenceEntry

A presence record for an agent viewing a document.

Members:

##### agentId

string

##### documentId

string

##### meta

Record<string, unknown> | undefined

Agent metadata (cursor position, color, etc.).

##### lastSeen

number

##### expiresAt

number

Expiry timestamp (ms since epoch).

#### ScratchpadMessage

ScratchpadMessage

A scratchpad message entry.

Members:

##### toAgentId

##### fromAgentId

string

Sender agent id.

##### payload

Record<string, unknown>

number

Expiry (ms since epoch). 0 = never expires.

#### SendScratchpadParams

SendScratchpadParams

Members:

##### toAgentId

string

##### fromAgentId

string

##### payload

Record<string, unknown>

##### ttlMs

number | undefined

TTL in ms. 0 = never expires. Defaults to 24h.

SearchResult

##### score

##### snippet

string | undefined

Matching snippet (optional).

#### SearchParams

SearchParams

Parameters for semantic search.

Members:

##### query

string

##### topK

number | undefined

Maximum results. Defaults to 10.

##### minScore

number | undefined

Minimum similarity score (0–1). Defaults to 0.0.

#### AgentPubkeyRecord

AgentPubkeyRecord

string

##### pubkeyHex

string

##### label

string | undefined
