number

##### revokedAt

number | undefined

If set, this key has been revoked.

#### AttachBlobParams

AttachBlobParams

Parameters for attaching a blob to a document.

Members:

##### docSlug

string

Document slug the blob is attached to.

##### name

string

User-visible attachment name (e.g. "diagram.png").

##### contentType

string

MIME content type.

##### data

Uint8Array<ArrayBufferLike> | Buffer<ArrayBufferLike>

Raw binary data.

##### uploadedBy

string

Agent performing the upload.

#### BlobAttachment

BlobAttachment

A stored blob attachment record.

Members:

##### id

string

##### docSlug

string

##### blobName

string

##### hash

string

##### size

number

##### contentType

string

##### uploadedBy

string

##### uploadedAt

number

#### BlobData

BlobData

Result of fetching a blob.

Members:

##### data

Buffer<ArrayBufferLike> | undefined

Raw blob bytes. Only present when fetched with includeData=true.

#### BlobRef

BlobRef

Changeset blob reference (bytes omitted — lazy pull).

Members:

##### blobName

string

##### hash

string

##### size

number

##### contentType

string

##### uploadedBy

string

##### uploadedAt

number

#### BlobOps

BlobOps

Blob storage and retrieval operations.

Members:

##### attachBlob()

(params: AttachBlobParams) => Promise<BlobAttachment>

Attach a binary blob to a document.  MUST compute SHA-256 hash of data and use it as the storage key. MUST validate the attachment name via llmtxt-core blob_name_validate. MUST enforce maxBlobSizeBytes (default 100MB). MUST apply LWW: if a blob with the same name already exists on the document,   it is soft-deleted and the new record becomes active. MUST NOT store duplicate bytes when hash already exists in the store   (content-addressed dedup within the same backend instance). MUST return the new BlobAttachment record.

##### getBlob()

(docSlug: string, blobName: string, opts?: { includeData?: boolean; }) => Promise<BlobData | null>

Retrieve a blob attachment, optionally including bytes.  MUST return null (not throw) if blobName is not attached to the document. MUST verify hash on read when includeData=true. Return BlobCorruptError if mismatch. Default: includeData = false (manifest metadata only).

##### listBlobs()

(docSlug: string) => Promise<BlobAttachment[]>

List all active (non-deleted) blob attachments for a document.  MUST return an empty array (not throw) when no blobs are attached. MUST NOT include bytes (manifest metadata only).

##### detachBlob()

(docSlug: string, blobName: string, detachedBy: string) => Promise<boolean>

Detach (soft-delete) a named blob from a document.  MUST return false (not throw) if no active attachment with blobName exists. MUST set deleted_at = now(). Actual byte storage is NOT cleaned up   (orphan collection is a deferred concern). MUST NOT affect other documents sharing the same blob hash.

##### fetchBlobByHash()

(hash: string) => Promise<Buffer | null>

Fetch blob bytes by hash directly (used during lazy sync pull).  MUST return null if no blob with this hash exists in the store. MUST verify hash on return. This method bypasses the manifest and is used by the sync layer.

#### DocumentOps

DocumentOps

Document CRUD operations.

Members:

##### createDocument()

(params: CreateDocumentParams) => Promise<Document>

Create a new document. MUST generate a unique slug from the title if not provided.

##### getDocument()

(id: string) => Promise<Document | null>

Retrieve a document by its id. MUST return null (not throw) when the document does not exist.

##### getDocumentBySlug()

(slug: string) => Promise<Document | null>

Retrieve a document by its slug. MUST return null (not throw) when the document does not exist.

##### listDocuments()

(params?: ListDocumentsParams) => Promise<ListResult<Document>>

List documents with optional filtering and cursor-based pagination.

##### deleteDocument()

(id: string) => Promise<boolean>

Delete a document and all associated data. MUST return false (not throw) if the document does not exist.

#### VersionOps

VersionOps

Version stack operations.

Members:

##### publishVersion()

(params: PublishVersionParams) => Promise<VersionEntry>

Publish a new version of a document. MUST compute and store the content hash via llmtxt-core hash_content. MUST increment the document's versionCount.

##### getVersion()

(documentId: string, versionNumber: number) => Promise<VersionEntry | null>

Retrieve a version entry by document id and version number. MUST return null when the version does not exist.

##### listVersions()

(documentId: string) => Promise<VersionEntry[]>

List all version entries for a document in ascending order.

##### transitionVersion()

(params: TransitionParams) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string; document?: Document; }>

Transition a document's lifecycle state. MUST validate the transition via sdk/lifecycle.ts validateTransition. MUST return an error result (not throw) for invalid transitions.

#### ApprovalOps

ApprovalOps

BFT approval operations.

Members:

##### submitSignedApproval()

(params: { documentId: string; versionNumber: number; reviewerId: string; status: "APPROVED" | "REJECTED"; reason?: string; signatureBase64: string; }) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string; result?: ApprovalResult; }>

Submit a signed approval (or rejection) for a document version. MUST verify the Ed25519 signature before persisting. MUST reject duplicate approvals from the same reviewer.

##### getApprovalProgress()

(documentId: string, versionNumber: number) => Promise<ApprovalResult>

Get current approval progress for a document version.

##### getApprovalPolicy()

(documentId: string) => Promise<ApprovalPolicy>

Get or set the approval policy for a document.

##### setApprovalPolicy()

(documentId: string, policy: ApprovalPolicy) => Promise<void>

#### EventOps

EventOps

Document event log operations.

Members:

##### appendEvent()

(params: AppendEventParams) => Promise<DocumentEvent>

Append an event to a document's event log. MUST emit the event on the local bus for subscribeStream consumers.

##### queryEvents()

(params: QueryEventsParams) => Promise<ListResult<DocumentEvent>>

Query the event log for a document with optional filtering.

##### subscribeStream()

(documentId: string) => AsyncIterable<DocumentEvent>

Subscribe to the event stream for a document. MUST return an AsyncIterable that yields events as they are appended. MUST clean up listeners when the consumer calls .return() or the iterator is GC'd. LocalBackend uses in-process EventEmitter; RemoteBackend uses SSE.

#### CrdtOps

CrdtOps

CRDT section operations.

Members:

##### applyCrdtUpdate()

(params: { documentId: string; sectionKey: string; updateBase64: string; agentId: string; }) => Promise<CrdtState>

Apply a Yjs binary update to a document section. MUST merge via llmtxt-core WASM merge_updates. MUST persist the raw update and update the section snapshot.

##### getCrdtState()

(documentId: string, sectionKey: string) => Promise<CrdtState | null>

Get the current CRDT state for a section. MUST return null when no state exists for the section.

##### subscribeSection()

(documentId: string, sectionKey: string) => AsyncIterable<CrdtUpdate>

Subscribe to CRDT updates for a document section. LocalBackend uses in-process EventEmitter; RemoteBackend uses WS.

#### LeaseOps

LeaseOps

Distributed lease operations.

Members:

##### acquireLease()

(params: AcquireLeaseParams) => Promise<Lease | null>

Acquire a lease on a resource. MUST fail (return null) if a non-expired lease exists for a different holder. MUST succeed (return existing) if the same holder re-acquires.

##### renewLease()

(resource: string, holder: string, ttlMs: number) => Promise<Lease | null>

Renew an existing lease, extending its TTL. MUST return null if the lease does not exist or is held by a different agent.

##### releaseLease()

(resource: string, holder: string) => Promise<boolean>

Release a lease immediately. MUST return false if the lease does not exist or holder mismatch.

##### getLease()

(resource: string) => Promise<Lease | null>

Get the current lease for a resource. Returns null if no active lease exists.

#### PresenceOps

PresenceOps

Presence (real-time who-is-viewing) operations.

Members:

##### joinPresence()

(documentId: string, agentId: string, meta?: Record<string, unknown>) => Promise<PresenceEntry>

Join or update presence for an agent on a document. Presence is NOT persisted across restarts — in-memory only.

##### leavePresence()

(documentId: string, agentId: string) => Promise<void>

Remove an agent from a document's presence.

##### listPresence()

(documentId: string) => Promise<PresenceEntry[]>

List all non-expired presence entries for a document.

##### heartbeatPresence()

(documentId: string, agentId: string) => Promise<void>

Refresh the lastSeen timestamp for an agent's presence.

#### ScratchpadOps

ScratchpadOps

Scratchpad ephemeral message operations.

Members:

##### sendScratchpad()

(params: SendScratchpadParams) => Promise<ScratchpadMessage>

Send a scratchpad message to an agent. Default TTL is 24 hours. exp=0 means never expires.

##### pollScratchpad()

(agentId: string, limit?: number) => Promise<ScratchpadMessage[]>

Poll scratchpad messages for an agent. MUST only return non-expired messages. MUST treat exp=0 entries as never-expired.

##### deleteScratchpadMessage()

(id: string, agentId: string) => Promise<boolean>

Delete scratchpad messages for an agent (after consumption).

#### A2AOps

A2AOps

A2A (Agent-to-Agent) inbox operations.

Members:

##### sendA2AMessage()

(params: { toAgentId: string; envelopeJson: string; ttlMs?: number; }) => Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string; message?: A2AMessage; }>

Deliver a signed A2A message to an agent's inbox. MUST verify the sender's Ed25519 signature before persisting. Default TTL is 48 hours.

##### pollA2AInbox()

(agentId: string, limit?: number, since?: number, order?: "asc" | "desc") => Promise<A2AMessage[]>

Poll messages from an agent's inbox. MUST only return non-expired messages.

Parameters:
- agentId: Recipient agent ID
- limit: Max messages to return (default 50, max 500)
- since: Only return messages with receivedAt  since (unix ms)
- order: Sort order: 'desc' (newest first, default) or 'asc' (oldest first)

##### deleteA2AMessage()

(id: string, agentId: string) => Promise<boolean>

Delete a message from an agent's inbox.

#### SearchOps

SearchOps

Semantic search operations.

Members:

##### indexDocument()

(documentId: string, content: string) => Promise<void>

Index a document version for semantic search. MUST compute an embedding vector and store it. SHOULD degrade gracefully when onnxruntime-node is not installed.

##### search()

(params: SearchParams) => Promise<SearchResult[]>

Perform semantic search across indexed documents. MUST return results sorted by cosine similarity descending. SHOULD return empty array (not throw) when embedding model is unavailable.

#### IdentityOps

IdentityOps

Agent identity and pubkey registry operations.

Members:

##### registerAgentPubkey()

(agentId: string, pubkeyHex: string, label?: string) => Promise<AgentPubkeyRecord>

Register an agent's public key. MUST be idempotent — registering the same key twice MUST NOT error.

##### lookupAgentPubkey()

(agentId: string) => Promise<AgentPubkeyRecord | null>

Look up an agent's registered public key. MUST return null when the agent has no registered key.

##### listAgentPubkeys()

(userId?: string) => Promise<AgentPubkeyRecord[]>

List all active (non-revoked) public key records. SHOULD support optional userId filter when the backend tracks ownership.

##### revokeAgentPubkey()

(agentId: string, pubkeyHex: string) => Promise<boolean>

Revoke an agent's public key. MUST set revokedAt on the key record.

##### recordSignatureNonce()

(agentId: string, nonce: string, ttlMs?: number) => Promise<boolean>

Record a signature nonce to prevent replay attacks. MUST fail if the nonce has already been recorded.

##### hasNonceBeenUsed()

(agentId: string, nonce: string) => Promise<boolean>

Check whether a nonce has already been used.

#### ContributorRecord

ContributorRecord

A contributor record tracking per-agent token attribution.

Members:

##### documentId

string

##### agentId

string

##### netTokens

number

Net token contribution (positive = added, negative = removed).

##### versionCount

number

Number of versions this agent has contributed.

##### lastContributedAt

number

#### ApiKey

ApiKey

#### CreateApiKeyParams

CreateApiKeyParams

Parameters for creating an API key.

Members:

##### userId

string

##### name

string

#### ApiKeyWithSecret

ApiKeyWithSecret

Full API key including secret — only returned at creation time.

Members:

##### secret

string

The full API key secret. Shown ONCE at creation; store securely.

#### ApprovalChainEntry

ApprovalChainEntry

An entry in the tamper-evident approval chain.

Members:

##### approvalId

string

##### reviewerId

string

##### status

"APPROVED" | "REJECTED"

##### atVersion

number

##### timestamp

number

##### chainHash

string

##### prevChainHash

string | null

##### sigHex

string | undefined

#### ApprovalChainResult

ApprovalChainResult

Result of verifying the approval chain for a document.

Members:

##### valid

boolean

##### length

number

##### firstInvalidAt

number | null

##### entries

ApprovalChainEntry[]

#### ContributorOps

ContributorOps

Contributor attribution operations.

Members:

##### listContributors()

(documentId: string) => Promise<ContributorRecord[]>

List all contributors for a document, ordered by net token contribution. MUST return an empty array (not throw) when no contributors exist.

#### BftOps

BftOps

BFT consensus chain operations.

Members:

##### getApprovalChain()

(documentId: string) => Promise<ApprovalChainResult>

Retrieve the tamper-evident approval chain for a document. MUST verify each chain link and report the first invalid position. MUST return an empty chain (not throw) when no approvals exist.

#### CollectionOps

CollectionOps

Collection CRUD and membership operations.

Members:

##### createCollection()

(params: CreateCollectionParams) => Promise<Collection>

Create a new collection. MUST generate a unique slug from the name if not provided.

##### getCollection()

(slug: string) => Promise<Collection | null>

Retrieve a collection by slug. MUST return null (not throw) when the collection does not exist.

##### listCollections()

(params?: ListCollectionsParams) => Promise<ListResult<Collection>>

List collections with optional owner filter and pagination.

##### addDocToCollection()

(collectionSlug: string, documentSlug: string, position?: number) => Promise<void>

Add a document to a collection at an optional position. MUST be idempotent — adding the same document twice MUST NOT error.

##### removeDocFromCollection()

(collectionSlug: string, documentSlug: string) => Promise<boolean>

Remove a document from a collection. MUST return false (not throw) when the document is not in the collection.

##### reorderCollection()

(collectionSlug: string, orderedSlugs: string[]) => Promise<void>

Reorder documents in a collection. MUST accept the full ordered list of document slugs as the new order.

##### exportCollection()

(collectionSlug: string) => Promise<CollectionExport>

Export a collection with all its documents' latest versions.

#### CrossDocOps

CrossDocOps

Cross-document link and graph operations.

Members:

##### createDocumentLink()

(params: CreateDocLinkParams) => Promise<DocumentLink>

Create a directed link between two documents. MUST be idempotent — duplicate links SHOULD be deduplicated.

##### getDocumentLinks()

(documentId: string) => Promise<DocumentLink[]>

Get all links originating from or pointing to a document.

##### deleteDocumentLink()

(documentId: string, linkId: string) => Promise<boolean>

Delete a document link. MUST return false (not throw) if the link does not exist.

##### getGlobalGraph()

(params?: { maxNodes?: number; }) => Promise<GraphResult>

Get the global document knowledge graph. SHOULD limit results for large backends (e.g., max 500 nodes).

#### WebhookOps

WebhookOps

Webhook registration and delivery operations.

Members:

##### createWebhook()

(params: CreateWebhookParams) => Promise<Webhook>

Register a new webhook endpoint. MUST generate a signing secret if not provided.

##### listWebhooks()

(userId: string) => Promise<Webhook[]>

List webhooks owned by a user.

##### deleteWebhook()

(id: string, userId: string) => Promise<boolean>

Delete a webhook. MUST verify ownership before deletion. MUST return false (not throw) if the webhook does not exist.

##### testWebhook()

(id: string) => Promise<WebhookTestResult>

Send a synthetic test delivery to a webhook. MUST deliver an HTTP POST to the webhook URL. SHOULD return the response status and body.

#### SignedUrlOps

SignedUrlOps

Signed URL (time-limited access token) operations.

Members:

##### createSignedUrl()

(params: CreateSignedUrlParams) => Promise<SignedUrl>

Create a time-limited signed access token for a document. ttlMs=0 means never expires. Default TTL is 24 hours.

##### verifySignedUrl()

(token: string) => Promise<{ documentId: string; permission: "read" | "write"; } | null>

Verify a signed URL token and return the associated document and permission. MUST return null when the token is invalid or expired.

#### AccessControlOps

AccessControlOps

Document access control operations.

Members:

##### getDocumentAccess()

(documentId: string) => Promise<AccessControlList>

Get the full access control list (visibility + role grants) for a document.

##### grantDocumentAccess()

(documentId: string, params: GrantAccessParams) => Promise<void>

Grant a user a role on a document. MUST be idempotent — granting the same role twice MUST NOT error.

##### revokeDocumentAccess()

(documentId: string, userId: string) => Promise<boolean>

Revoke all role grants for a user on a document. MUST return false (not throw) when the user has no grant.

##### setDocumentVisibility()

(documentId: string, visibility: DocumentVisibility) => Promise<void>

Set the document's visibility level.

#### OrganizationOps

OrganizationOps

Organization management operations.

Members:

##### createOrganization()

(params: CreateOrgParams) => Promise<Organization>

Create a new organization. MUST generate a unique slug from the name if not provided.

##### getOrganization()

(slug: string) => Promise<Organization | null>

Retrieve an organization by slug. MUST return null (not throw) when the organization does not exist.

##### listOrganizations()

(userId: string) => Promise<Organization[]>

List organizations the user belongs to or owns.

##### addOrgMember()

(orgSlug: string, userId: string, role?: string) => Promise<void>

Add a user to an organization. MUST be idempotent — adding the same member twice MUST NOT error.

##### removeOrgMember()

(orgSlug: string, userId: string) => Promise<boolean>

Remove a user from an organization. MUST return false (not throw) when the user is not a member.

#### ApiKeyOps

ApiKeyOps

API key management operations.

Members:

##### createApiKey()

(params: CreateApiKeyParams) => Promise<ApiKeyWithSecret>

Create a new API key for a user. MUST return the full secret exactly once in ApiKeyWithSecret. Subsequent retrieval via listApiKeys MUST NOT return the secret.

##### listApiKeys()

(userId: string) => Promise<ApiKey[]>

List all active API keys for a user (without secrets).

##### deleteApiKey()

(id: string, userId: string) => Promise<boolean>

Revoke an API key. MUST verify ownership before revoking. MUST return false (not throw) if the key does not exist.

##### rotateApiKey()

(id: string, userId: string) => Promise<ApiKeyWithSecret>

Rotate an API key: revoke the old one and issue a new one with the same name. MUST verify ownership before rotation.

#### Backend

Backend

Backend — the complete LLMtxt persistence and coordination interface.  Implementations MUST satisfy all sub-interfaces. Consumers of this interface SHOULD NOT depend on any implementation-specific methods.  Both LocalBackend (packages/llmtxt/src/local/) and RemoteBackend (packages/llmtxt/src/remote/) implement this interface.

Example:
  import { LocalBackend } from 'llmtxt/local';
  const backend: Backend = new LocalBackend({ storagePath: './.llmtxt' });
  await backend.open();
  const doc = await backend.createDocument({ title: 'My Doc', createdBy: 'agent-1' });
  await backend.close();

Members:

##### open()

() => Promise<void>

Open the backend connection / apply migrations. MUST be called before any other method. MUST be idempotent (calling open twice MUST NOT error).

##### close()

() => Promise<void>

Close the backend, releasing resources (DB handles, timers, sockets). MUST stop all background reapers and interval timers. MUST be safe to call multiple times.

##### config

BackendConfig

The BackendConfig this instance was constructed with.

#### ExportDocumentParams

ExportDocumentParams

Parameters for exporting a single document.

Members:

##### slug

string

URL-safe document slug.

##### format

ExportFormat

Export format.

##### outputPath

string

Absolute or relative path to write the output file.

##### includeMetadata

boolean | undefined

Whether to include metadata (frontmatter/structured fields). Default true.

##### sign

boolean | undefined

If true, sign the export manifest with the local Ed25519 identity. Default false.

#### ExportDocumentResult

ExportDocumentResult

Result returned by exportDocument().

Members:

##### filePath

string

Absolute path of the written file.

##### slug

string

Slug of the exported document.

##### version

number

Version number exported.

##### fileHash

string

SHA-256 hex of the written file bytes.

##### byteCount

number

Number of bytes written.

##### exportedAt

string

ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of export.

##### signatureHex

string | null

Ed25519 signature hex over fileHash, if sign=true. Null otherwise.

#### ExportAllParams

ExportAllParams

Parameters for exporting all documents to a directory.

Members:

##### format

ExportFormat

##### outputDir

string

Directory to write files into. One file per document, named `<slug>.<ext>`.

##### state

string | undefined

Filter by lifecycle state. If absent, exports all documents.

##### includeMetadata

boolean | undefined

##### sign

boolean | undefined

#### ExportAllResult

ExportAllResult

Result returned by exportAll().

Members:

##### exported

ExportDocumentResult[]

##### skipped

{ slug: string; reason: string; }[]

##### totalCount

number

##### failedCount

number

#### ImportDocumentParams

ImportDocumentParams

Parameters for importing a document from a file.

Members:

##### filePath

string

Path to the file to import (.md, .json, .txt, .llmtxt).

##### importedBy

string

Agent performing the import.

##### onConflict

"new_version" | "create" | undefined

Conflict strategy when a document with the same slug already exists. 'new_version': publish the imported content as a new version (default). 'create': fail with ExportError('SLUG_EXISTS') if the slug already exists.

#### ImportDocumentResult

ImportDocumentResult

Result returned by importDocument().

Members:

##### action

"created" | "version_appended"

Whether a new document was created or a version was appended.

##### slug

string

##### documentId

string

##### versionNumber

number

##### contentHash

string

#### ExportOps

ExportOps

Document export operations.

Members:

##### exportDocument()

(params: ExportDocumentParams) => Promise<ExportDocumentResult>

Export a single document to a file on disk.  MUST resolve slug to a document; MUST throw ExportError('DOC_NOT_FOUND') if absent. MUST fetch the latest version content from the backend. MUST write the file atomically (write to .tmp then rename). MUST return the SHA-256 hash of the written bytes. MUST create intermediate directories via mkdirSync recursive. MUST NOT mutate any document, version, or event row in the database.

##### exportAll()

(params: ExportAllParams) => Promise<ExportAllResult>

Export all documents to a directory.  MUST iterate all documents (paginating via listDocuments). MUST call exportDocument for each; individual failures MUST be collected in skipped, not thrown. MUST write each file as . inside outputDir.

##### importDocument()

(params: ImportDocumentParams) => Promise<ImportDocumentResult>

Import a document from a file on disk.  MUST parse frontmatter from .md and .llmtxt files. MUST parse the `content` field from .json files. MUST read raw body from .txt files (no frontmatter). MUST NOT silently ignore a frontmatter content_hash that does not match the body. MUST create a new document if no document with the slug exists. MUST publish a new version if the document exists and onConflict='new_version'. MUST return ExportError('SLUG_EXISTS') if document exists and onConflict='create'.

##### getChangesSince()

(dbVersion: bigint) => Promise<Uint8Array>

Returns all changes made to this database since `dbVersion`.  `dbVersion = 0n` returns the full change history. Returns an empty Uint8Array (not null) when no changes exist.  The changeset is the cr-sqlite binary wire format as defined in docs/specs/P2-cr-sqlite.md §3.3 (DR-P2-03).

##### applyChanges()

(changeset: Uint8Array) => Promise<bigint>

Applies a changeset received from a peer.  MUST be idempotent: applying the same changeset twice produces identical state.  For rows affecting section_crdt_states.crdt_state, the implementation MUST perform application-level Loro merge instead of cr-sqlite LWW (DR-P2-04).

Returns: The new local db_version after applying the changeset.

#### FrontmatterMeta

FrontmatterMeta

Structured input for `canonicalFrontmatter`.  Mirrors `FrontmatterMeta` in `crates/llmtxt-core/src/canonical.rs`. Contributors are sorted inside this function — callers MUST NOT pre-sort.

Members:

##### title

string

Document title (UTF-8, double-quoted in output).

##### slug

string

URL-safe slug.

##### version

number

Integer version number of the exported state.

##### state

string

Lifecycle state string (e.g. "DRAFT", "APPROVED").

##### contributors

string[]

Agent IDs — sorted lexicographically by this function.

##### content_hash

string

SHA-256 hex of the body content (64 lowercase chars).

##### exported_at

string

ISO 8601 UTC timestamp with millisecond precision.

#### DocumentExportState

DocumentExportState

Self-contained document snapshot passed to all format serializers.  All fields required by the canonical frontmatter schema are present at the top level; additional fields feed the JSON format.

Members:

##### title

string

Human-readable document title.

##### slug

string

URL-safe slug.

##### version

number

Version number of the exported state (integer, 1-based).

##### state

string

Lifecycle state string (DRAFT | REVIEW | LOCKED | ARCHIVED).

##### contributors

string[]

Agent IDs that have contributed to this document. Formatters MUST sort these lexicographically before serialization.

##### contentHash

string

SHA-256 hex of the body content (64 lowercase hex chars). Callers compute this; formatters embed it verbatim.

##### exportedAt

string

ISO 8601 UTC timestamp with millisecond precision. Injected by the caller so that determinism is achievable across repeated calls.

##### content

string

Full body content of the exported version.

##### labels

string[] | null | undefined

Arbitrary metadata labels. Present if available; omitted or null otherwise.

##### createdBy

string | null | undefined

Agent that created the document.

##### createdAt

number | null | undefined

Creation timestamp (Unix milliseconds).

##### updatedAt

number | null | undefined

Last-modified timestamp (Unix milliseconds).

##### versionCount

number | null | undefined

Total number of versions for this document.

##### chainRef

string | null | undefined

BFT approval chain hash from `getApprovalChain`. Null when no approvals exist or when CRDT state is unavailable (T451 stub).

#### ExportOpts

ExportOpts

Options controlling format serializer behaviour.  All fields are optional. Formatters use sensible defaults when absent.

Members:

##### includeMetadata

boolean | undefined

Whether to include metadata (frontmatter / structured fields). Defaults to `true`.  When `false`: - `formatMarkdown` emits body only (no frontmatter fences). - `formatLlmtxt` behaves identically to `formatMarkdown` with `includeMetadata: false`. - `formatJson` ignores this flag (JSON format always includes metadata). - `formatTxt` ignores this flag (plain-text format never includes metadata).

#### ParsedImport

ParsedImport

Members:

##### slug

string

URL-safe slug (from frontmatter, or derived from filename).

##### title

string

Human-readable title (from frontmatter, or derived from filename).

##### content

string

Raw body content.

##### expectedContentHash

string | null

Expected SHA-256 hex of content, if present in frontmatter. Callers MUST verify this matches the actual body before importing.

#### PeerTransport

PeerTransport

PeerTransport — transport abstraction for P2P mesh (P3 spec §4.1).  Implementations MUST complete Ed25519 mutual handshake before any changeset data is exchanged. Unauthenticated peers MUST be rejected.

Members:

##### type

string

Transport type identifier (e.g., `"unix"`, `"http"`).

##### listen()

(onChangeset: (peerId: string, changeset: Uint8Array) => void) => Promise<void>

Listen for incoming connections.  MUST complete Ed25519 mutual handshake before calling `onChangeset()`. MUST reject connections that fail the handshake. MUST call `onChangeset()` for each received, authenticated changeset.

##### sendChangeset()

(peerId: string, peerAddress: string, changeset: Uint8Array) => Promise<void>

Send a changeset to a specific peer.  MUST complete Ed25519 mutual handshake before sending any data. MUST throw `PeerUnreachableError` if the peer is unreachable after `MAX_RETRIES` attempts.

##### close()

() => Promise<void>

Graceful shutdown — close all open connections and stop listening.

#### TransportIdentity

TransportIdentity

Local identity used by the transport layer for handshakes.

Members:

##### agentId

string

Hex-encoded SHA-256 of the public key bytes.

##### publicKey

Uint8Array<ArrayBufferLike>

32-byte Ed25519 public key.

##### privateKey

Uint8Array<ArrayBufferLike>

32-byte Ed25519 private key seed.

#### PeerInfo

PeerInfo

Members:

##### agentId

string

Hex-encoded public key hash (agentId).

##### address

string

Transport address (e.g. "unix:/tmp/agent.sock" or "http://host:port").

##### pubkeyBase64

string

Base64-encoded Ed25519 public key.

#### PeerRegistry

PeerRegistry

Members:

##### discover()

() => Promise<PeerInfo[]>

Returns the current set of discovered peers (excluding self).

##### markInactive()

(agentId: string) => void

Mark a peer as temporarily inactive after repeated failures.

#### PeerSyncState

PeerSyncState

In-memory peer sync state. Persisted externally via llmtxt_mesh_state table.

Members:

##### lastSyncVersion

bigint

##### failureCount

number

##### lastFailureAt

number | undefined

#### SyncEngineOptions

SyncEngineOptions

Members:

##### backend

Backend

##### transport

PeerTransport

##### discovery

PeerRegistry

##### identity

AgentIdentity

##### syncIntervalMs

number | undefined

##### maxPeerFailures

number | undefined

Maximum consecutive failures before a peer is marked inactive.

#### A2AEnvelope

A2AEnvelope

A2A message envelope. Spec §7.1:    type, from, to, payload, sig, sentAt   sig = base64-encoded Ed25519 signature over canonical JSON of        type, from, to, payload, sentAt  (fields alphabetically sorted,       no trailing whitespace).

Members:

##### type

"a2a"

##### from

string

##### to

string

##### payload

Record<string, unknown>

##### sig

string

##### sentAt

string

#### MeshMessengerOptions

MeshMessengerOptions

Members:

##### identity

AgentIdentity

##### transport

PeerTransport

##### discovery

PeerRegistry

##### onMessage

((envelope: A2AEnvelope) => void) | undefined

Handler called when a valid, verified A2A message arrives for THIS agent.

#### PostgresBackendConfig

PostgresBackendConfig

Extended config for PostgresBackend.

Members:

##### connectionString

string | undefined

PostgreSQL connection string. MUST be in the format: postgresql://user:passhost:5432/dbname Defaults to DATABASE_URL environment variable.

##### maxConnections

number | undefined

Maximum number of connections in the pool. Defaults to 10.

#### PgBackendHandle

PgBackendHandle

Members:

##### adapter

PgContractAdapter

The contract-test-ready adapter.

##### cleanup

() => Promise<void>

Drop the test schema and end connections.

#### BlobChangeset

BlobChangeset

Extended changeset type carrying optional blob references.  This wraps the existing cr-sqlite binary changeset (Uint8Array) with a separate `blobs` array that carries manifest metadata — never raw bytes. The blob bytes are pulled lazily on first `getBlob(includeData=true)` call.

Members:

##### crsqlChangeset

Uint8Array<ArrayBufferLike>

The cr-sqlite binary changeset (may be empty Uint8Array).

##### blobs

BlobRef[]

Blob references for all blob operations in the transaction window.

#### ApplyBlobChangesetResult

ApplyBlobChangesetResult

Result of applying a BlobChangeset.

Members:

##### applied

number

Number of blob refs successfully applied (winners inserted).

##### discarded

number

Number of blob refs that lost LWW and were discarded.

##### pendingFetches

string[]

Hashes scheduled for lazy background pull.

#### BlobRefWithDocSlug

BlobRefWithDocSlug

Extended BlobRef used internally in the sync layer. docSlug is required for manifest writes but is omitted from the public BlobRef spec interface (which is scoped to a document context by the caller).

Members:

##### docSlug

string

#### PeerRegistration

PeerRegistration

Raw format of a `.peer` file written by a running agent (P3 spec §3.2).  All fields are required. Peer files missing any field are rejected.

Members:

##### agentId

string

Hex-encoded SHA-256 of the agent's Ed25519 public key bytes.

##### transport

string

Transport address string. Format: `unix:<absolute-path>` or `http://host:port`.

##### pubkey

string

Base64-encoded 32-byte Ed25519 public key.

##### capabilities

string[]

Capabilities advertised by this peer.

##### startedAt

string

ISO-8601 timestamp when the agent started.

#### PeerInfo

PeerInfo

A validated peer — returned from `discover()` and `loadStaticConfig()`. All entries here have been security-checked.

Members:

##### active

boolean

Whether the peer is considered active (startedAt within PEER_TTL_MS).

#### EventPublisher

EventPublisher<T>

An object that can publish typed events onto a named channel.  Implementations MUST be synchronous-safe — callers do not await publish().

Members:

##### publish()

(channel: string, event: T) => void

Emit `event` on `channel`.  All active subscribers on `channel` MUST receive the event in FIFO order. Delivery to a slow subscriber MUST NOT block other subscribers.

#### EventSubscriber

EventSubscriber<T>

An object that can return an async iterable of typed events from a channel.  The iterable MUST yield events in the order they were published. Calling `return()` on the iterator MUST unsubscribe from the channel.

Members:

##### subscribe()

(channel: string) => AsyncIterable<T>

Return an `AsyncIterable<T>` that yields every event published on `channel` after the subscription is opened.  Past events (before the subscription was opened) MUST NOT be replayed unless the implementation explicitly supports seek/cursor semantics.

#### EventStream

EventStream<T>

A bidirectional event stream that can both publish and subscribe.

#### EmitterLike

EmitterLike

Minimal EventEmitter surface required by makeEventStream.

Members:

##### on()

(event: string, listener: (...args: unknown[]) => void) => void

##### off()

(event: string, listener: (...args: unknown[]) => void) => void

#### DocumentEventBusLike

DocumentEventBusLike

Shape of the document event bus used by PostgresBackend (injected from apps/backend realtime layer).  Uses a single `'document'` channel and emits objects shaped as `{ type, slug, documentId, timestamp, actor, data }`.

Members:

##### on()

(event: "document", listener: (payload: unknown) => void) => void

##### off()

(event: "document", listener: (payload: unknown) => void) => void

#### PresenceEntry

PresenceEntry

Presence state broadcast by an agent to all connected peers. Matches the JSON structure in spec §6.1.

Members:

##### agentId

string

Hex-encoded public key hash.

##### documentId

string | null

Document the agent is currently editing (null if idle).

##### sectionId

string | null

Section within the document (null if not section-level).

##### updatedAt

string

ISO-8601 timestamp of last update.

##### ttl

number

Time-to-live in seconds. Entries expire after this many seconds.

##### receivedAt

number

Wall-clock epoch ms when this entry was received/created locally.

#### PresenceManagerOptions

PresenceManagerOptions

Members:

##### agentId

string

##### transport

PeerTransport

##### discovery

PeerRegistry

##### ttlSeconds

number | undefined

Default TTL in seconds (spec default: 30).

##### broadcastIntervalMs

number | undefined

Broadcast interval in ms (spec: 10 s).

##### rateLimitWindowMs

number | undefined

Rate limit window in ms — max 1 message per peer per window (spec: 5 s).

##### initialDocumentId

string | null | undefined

Current document/section the agent is editing (updated via setPresence).

##### initialSectionId

string | null | undefined

#### ContributionReceipt

ContributionReceipt

Contribution Receipt: auditable proof of work performed during a session.  RFC 2119 requirement: All fields are mandatory except signature (which is mandatory only for RemoteBackend).

Members:

##### sessionId

string

Session ID (128-bit random, unguessable).

##### agentId

string

Agent identity ID (must match authenticated identity in backend).

##### documentIds

string[]

Unique document IDs written during the session (sorted for determinism).

##### eventCount

number

Total successful write operations performed via contribute().

##### sessionDurationMs

number

Session duration in milliseconds (closedAt - openedAt).

##### openedAt

string

ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of session open.

##### closedAt

string

ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of session close.

##### signature

string | undefined

Ed25519 signature over the canonical receipt payload. MUST be present when backend is RemoteBackend (cross-network). MAY be omitted for LocalBackend (same-process).  Signature covers: SHA-256(sessionId + agentId + documentIds.sort().join(',') +                   eventCount + openedAt + closedAt)  Stub for now — T461 will add Ed25519 signing to AgentSession.

#### CloseStepError

CloseStepError

Error raised when one or more close() teardown steps fail while the session still reaches the Closed state. Callers can inspect `errors` for details and `receipt` for the partial receipt.

Members:

##### step

string

##### error

Error

#### AgentSessionOptions

AgentSessionOptions

AgentSessionOptions: constructor configuration.

Members:

##### backend

Backend

Backend to operate through (LocalBackend or RemoteBackend). Typed as Backend to enable proper method calls; consumers pass concrete implementations (LocalBackend or RemoteBackend).

##### agentId

string

Agent identity. MUST match the authenticated identity registered in the backend's identity primitives.

##### sessionId

string | undefined

Cryptographically random session ID (128-bit entropy minimum). If omitted, AgentSession generates one using crypto.randomUUID(). MUST be unguessable; predictable IDs allow session hijacking.

##### label

string | undefined

Human-readable label for this session. Used in receipts. Defaults to agentId + timestamp ISO string.

#### PostgresRowChange

PostgresRowChange

A single row-level change captured from Postgres.  In a full implementation this would be populated via Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY on a changes table, or via logical decoding (pg_logical_emit_message).

Members:

##### table

string

Name of the table that changed (e.g., "documents", "versions").

##### op

"INSERT" | "UPDATE" | "DELETE"

Operation type.

##### newRow

Record<string, unknown> | null

The row data after the change (null for DELETE).

##### oldRow

Record<string, unknown> | null

The row data before the change (null for INSERT).

##### txid

number

Postgres transaction ID (xid) — used as a logical "db_version".

##### changedAt

string

ISO-8601 timestamp of the change.

#### PostgresChangeset

PostgresChangeset

Wire format for a PostgresChangeset batch.  TODO: Align with the binary format produced by LocalBackend.getChangesSince() once the cr-sqlite changeset schema is stabilised in P2.6.

Members:

##### txids

number[]

Postgres transaction IDs included in this batch.

##### changes

PostgresRowChange[]

Serialized row changes (JSON for now; binary in full implementation).

##### maxTxid

number

The highest txid in this batch (used as "sinceVersion" by the receiver).

#### PostgresChangesetAdapterOptions

PostgresChangesetAdapterOptions

Options for constructing a PostgresChangesetAdapter.

Members:

##### db

unknown

Postgres client or connection pool used for querying row changes.  TODO: Replace `unknown` with the concrete postgres.js client type once the full implementation is written.

##### sinceXid

number | undefined

The last synced Postgres transaction ID (xid) for a given peer. Rows with txid  sinceXid will be included in the next changeset. Defaults to 0 (full snapshot).

#### MeshChangesetRouteOptions

MeshChangesetRouteOptions

Options for the POST /mesh/changeset route handler.

Members:

##### adapter

PostgresChangesetAdapter

PostgresChangesetAdapter instance wired to the PostgresBackend.

##### maxChangesetBytes

number | undefined

Maximum changeset size in bytes (default: 10 MB per P3 spec §10).

#### MeshChangesetResult

MeshChangesetResult

Generic POST /mesh/changeset handler result.  The route handler is framework-agnostic: the caller (Hono/Express/etc.) provides the request body and receives a structured result to render.

Members:

##### status

number

HTTP status code to return.

##### body

Record<string, unknown>

Response body as an object (caller serializes to JSON).

##### delta

Uint8Array<ArrayBufferLike> | undefined

Delta changeset bytes to return in the response body (bidirectional sync).

#### A2AEnvelope

A2AEnvelope

Canonical A2A message envelope (matches crates/llmtxt-core/src/a2a.rs).

Members:

##### from

string

##### to

string

##### nonce

string

##### timestamp_ms

number

##### signature

string

##### content_type

string

##### payload

string

Base64-encoded payload bytes.

#### BuildA2AOptions

BuildA2AOptions

Options for building an A2A message.

Members:

##### from

string

##### to

string

##### payload

unknown

##### contentType

string | undefined

##### identity

AgentIdentity

##### nowMs

number | undefined

##### nonce

string | undefined

#### InboxDeliveryResponse

InboxDeliveryResponse

Response from POST /agents/:id/inbox.

Members:

##### delivered

boolean

##### to

string

##### from

string

##### nonce

string

##### sig_verified

boolean

##### expires_at

number

#### InboxMessage

InboxMessage

Message in agent inbox response.

Members:

##### id

string

##### from

string

##### to

string

##### envelope

A2AEnvelope

##### received_at

number

##### expires_at

number

##### read

boolean

#### InboxPollResponse

InboxPollResponse

Response from GET /agents/:id/inbox.

Members:

##### messages

InboxMessage[]

##### count

number

#### SignedApprovalEnvelope

SignedApprovalEnvelope

Signed approval envelope ready to POST to /bft/approve.

Members:

##### status

BFTApprovalStatus

##### sig_hex

string

##### canonical_payload

string

##### comment

string | undefined

#### BFTApprovalResponse

BFTApprovalResponse

Response from POST /documents/:slug/bft/approve.

Members:

##### slug

string

##### approvalId

string

##### status

BFTApprovalStatus

##### sigVerified

boolean

##### chainHash

string

##### bftF

number

##### quorum

number

##### currentApprovals

number

##### quorumReached

boolean

#### BFTStatusResponse

BFTStatusResponse

Response from GET /documents/:slug/bft/status.

Members:

##### slug

string

##### bftF

number

##### quorum

number

##### currentApprovals

number

##### quorumReached

boolean

##### approvers

string[]

#### ChainVerificationResponse

ChainVerificationResponse

Response from GET /documents/:slug/chain.

Members:

##### valid

boolean

##### length

number

##### firstInvalidAt

number | null

##### slug

string

#### ScratchpadMessage

ScratchpadMessage

A scratchpad message as returned by the API.

Members:

##### id

string

##### agent_id

string

##### content

string

##### content_type

string

##### thread_id

string | undefined

##### sig_hex

string | undefined

##### timestamp_ms

number

#### SendScratchpadOptions

SendScratchpadOptions

Options for sending a scratchpad message.

Members:

##### content

string

Message content body.

##### contentType

string | undefined

MIME content type (default: "text/plain").

##### threadId

string | undefined

Optional thread identifier for reply chains.

##### identity

AgentIdentity | undefined

Agent identity for signing (optional — unsigned if omitted).

##### agentId

string | undefined

Agent ID to include in the request (required if identity provided).

#### ReadScratchpadOptions

ReadScratchpadOptions

Options for reading scratchpad messages.

Members:

##### lastId

string | undefined

Return messages after this stream ID.

##### threadId

string | undefined

Filter by thread.

##### limit

number | undefined

Maximum number of messages to return. Default 100.

### Types

#### AwarenessEventType

AwarenessEventType

Events emitted when awareness state changes.

#### Unsubscribe

Unsubscribe

Unsubscribe function returned by onAwarenessChange.

#### SectionAlignment

SectionAlignment

How a section from version A maps to version B.

#### AuditAction

AuditAction

Action type for audit logging. Represents the type of operation that occurred.

#### LinkType

"references" | "depends_on" | "derived_from" | "supersedes" | "related"

Type for a cross-document link relationship.

#### ContentFormat

ContentFormat

Supported content formats.

#### DocumentMode

DocumentMode

Lifecycle state for collaborative documents.

#### AttachmentAccessMode

AttachmentAccessMode

Attachment fetch mode supported by the bridge/API layer.

#### AttachmentSharingMode

AttachmentSharingMode

Share state persisted by the API layer for an attachment.

#### Permission

Permission

Fine-grained permission on a document. Canonical definition; mirrors crates/llmtxt-core::rbac::Permission.

#### DocumentRole

DocumentRole

Role a user holds on a specific document. Canonical definition; mirrors crates/llmtxt-core::rbac::DocumentRole.

#### OrgRole

OrgRole

Role a user holds within an organisation. Canonical definition; mirrors crates/llmtxt-core::rbac::OrgRole.

#### DocumentEventType

DocumentEventType

Discriminant for document lifecycle events emitted by the event bus.  Consumers should use this type when subscribing to the bus or when filtering events in webhook handlers.

#### ResignResult

ReshareResult

Backward-compatible alias. Prefer `ReshareResult`.

#### Unsubscribe

Unsubscribe

Function to call to unsubscribe and close the WebSocket connection.

#### PredefinedSchemaName

"prompt-v1"

Union of all registered predefined schema name strings.

Derived from the keys of the `predefinedSchemas` registry. Currently resolves to `"prompt-v1"`.

#### JsonFormat

string | number | boolean | Record<string, unknown> | unknown[] | null

Inferred TypeScript type for any valid JSON value (object, array, primitive).

Derived from `jsonFormatSchema`. Represents the union of all JSON-compatible types: `Record<string, unknown>`, `unknown[]`, `string`, `number`, `boolean`, and `null`.

#### TextFormat

string

Inferred TypeScript type for plain text content.

Derived from `textFormatSchema`. Resolves to `string`.

#### MarkdownFormat

string

Inferred TypeScript type for markdown content (string alias).

Derived from `markdownFormatSchema`. Resolves to `string`.

#### PromptV1

{ messages: { role: "system" | "user" | "assistant"; content: string; }[]; system?: string | undefined; temperature?: number | undefined; max_tokens?: number | undefined; }

Inferred TypeScript type for the standard LLM prompt format.

Derived from `promptV1Schema`. Includes optional `system` string, required `messages` array, and optional `temperature` and `max_tokens`.

#### PromptMessage

{ role: "system" | "user" | "assistant"; content: string; }

Inferred TypeScript type for a single prompt message with role and content.

Derived from `promptMessageSchema`. Each message has a `role` (`"system"`, `"user"`, or `"assistant"`) and a `content` string.

#### CompressRequest

{ content: string; format: "text" | "markdown" | "json"; schema?: string | undefined; metadata?: Record<string, unknown> | undefined; }

Inferred TypeScript type for content compression request payloads.

Derived from `compressRequestSchema`. Includes required `content`, optional `format`, optional `schema`, and optional `metadata`.

#### DecompressRequest

{ slug: string; }

Inferred TypeScript type for content decompression request payloads.

Derived from `decompressRequestSchema`. Contains a required `slug` field.

#### SearchRequest

{ query: string; slugs: string[]; }

Inferred TypeScript type for content search request payloads.

Derived from `searchRequestSchema`. Contains a required `query` string and a non-empty `slugs` array.

#### DocumentState

DocumentState

Lifecycle state of a collaborative document.

#### ApprovalStatus

ApprovalStatus

Status of an individual review.

#### StorageType

StorageType

How document content is stored.

#### CompressionMethod

CompressionMethod

Compression method used for stored content.

#### Unsubscribe

Unsubscribe

Call to close the SSE subscription.

#### TopologyMode

TopologyMode

The three supported deployment topologies.  - `standalone` — one agent, one local .db file, zero network dependency. - `hub-spoke` — N ephemeral or persistent spokes connected to a central hub. - `mesh` — N persistent peers syncing via P2P transport (T386).

#### TopologyConfig

TopologyConfig

Discriminated union of all topology configs, keyed on the `topology` field.

#### DocumentVisibility

DocumentVisibility

Document visibility level.

#### ExportErrorCode

ExportErrorCode

Error codes for the document export subsystem.

#### ExportFormat

ExportFormat

Supported document export formats.

#### Document

{ slug: string; expiresAt: number | null; createdAt: number; id: string; title: string; state: string; createdBy: string; visibility: string; updatedAt: number; versionCount: number; labelsJson: string; eventSeqCounter: number; bftF: number; requiredApprovals: number; approvalTimeoutMs: number; }

#### NewDocument

{ slug: string; createdAt: number; id: string; title: string; createdBy: string; updatedAt: number; expiresAt?: number | null | undefined; state?: string | undefined; visibility?: string | undefined; versionCount?: number | undefined; labelsJson?: string | undefined; eventSeqCounter?: number | undefined; bftF?: number | undefined; requiredApprovals?: number | undefined; approvalTimeoutMs?: number | undefined; }

#### Version

{ versionNumber: number; changelog: string | null; createdAt: number; id: string; createdBy: string | null; documentId: string; compressedData: unknown; contentHash: string; tokenCount: number | null; patchText: string | null; baseVersion: number | null; storageType: string; storageKey: string | null; }

#### NewVersion

{ versionNumber: number; createdAt: number; id: string; documentId: string; contentHash: string; changelog?: string | null | undefined; createdBy?: string | null | undefined; compressedData?: unknown; tokenCount?: number | null | undefined; patchText?: string | null | undefined; baseVersion?: number | null | undefined; storageType?: string | undefined; storageKey?: string | null | undefined; }

#### StateTransition

{ id: string; documentId: string; fromState: string; toState: string; changedBy: string; changedAt: number; reason: string | null; atVersion: number; }

#### NewStateTransition

{ id: string; documentId: string; fromState: string; toState: string; changedBy: string; changedAt: number; atVersion: number; reason?: string | null | undefined; }

#### Approval

{ timestamp: number; id: string; bftF: number; documentId: string; reason: string | null; atVersion: number; reviewerId: string; status: string; sigHex: string | null; canonicalPayload: string | null; chainHash: string | null; prevChainHash: string | null; }

#### NewApproval

{ timestamp: number; id: string; documentId: string; atVersion: number; reviewerId: string; status: string; bftF?: number | undefined; reason?: string | null | undefined; sigHex?: string | null | undefined; canonicalPayload?: string | null | undefined; chainHash?: string | null | undefined; prevChainHash?: string | null | undefined; }

#### SectionCrdtState

{ updatedAt: number; documentId: string; sectionId: string; clock: number; crdtState: unknown; }

#### NewSectionCrdtState

{ updatedAt: number; documentId: string; sectionId: string; crdtState: unknown; clock?: number | undefined; }

#### SectionCrdtUpdate

{ createdAt: number; id: string; documentId: string; sectionId: string; updateBlob: unknown; clientId: string; seq: number; }

#### NewSectionCrdtUpdate

{ createdAt: number; id: string; documentId: string; sectionId: string; updateBlob: unknown; clientId: string; seq: number; }

#### DocumentEvent

{ createdAt: number; id: string; documentId: string; seq: number; eventType: string; actorId: string; payloadJson: string; idempotencyKey: string | null; prevHash: string | null; }

#### NewDocumentEvent

{ createdAt: number; id: string; documentId: string; seq: number; eventType: string; actorId: string; payloadJson?: string | undefined; idempotencyKey?: string | null | undefined; prevHash?: string | null | undefined; }

#### AgentPubkey

{ agentId: string; createdAt: number; id: string; pubkeyHex: string; label: string | null; revokedAt: number | null; }

#### NewAgentPubkey

{ agentId: string; createdAt: number; id: string; pubkeyHex: string; label?: string | null | undefined; revokedAt?: number | null | undefined; }

#### AgentSignatureNonce

{ expiresAt: number; agentId: string; nonce: string; firstSeen: number; }

#### NewAgentSignatureNonce

{ expiresAt: number; agentId: string; nonce: string; firstSeen: number; }

#### SectionLease

{ expiresAt: number; id: string; resource: string; holder: string; acquiredAt: number; }

#### NewSectionLease

{ expiresAt: number; id: string; resource: string; holder: string; acquiredAt: number; }

#### AgentInboxMessage

{ createdAt: number; id: string; toAgentId: string; envelopeJson: string; exp: number; }

#### NewAgentInboxMessage

{ createdAt: number; id: string; toAgentId: string; envelopeJson: string; exp: number; }

#### ScratchpadEntry

{ createdAt: number; id: string; payloadJson: string; toAgentId: string; exp: number; fromAgentId: string; }

#### NewScratchpadEntry

{ createdAt: number; id: string; toAgentId: string; exp: number; fromAgentId: string; payloadJson?: string | undefined; }

#### SectionEmbedding

{ versionNumber: number; createdAt: number; id: string; documentId: string; sectionKey: string; embeddingBlob: unknown; dimensions: number; modelId: string; }

#### NewSectionEmbedding

{ versionNumber: number; createdAt: number; id: string; documentId: string; embeddingBlob: unknown; dimensions: number; modelId: string; sectionKey?: string | undefined; }

#### BlobAttachment

{ id: string; docSlug: string; blobName: string; hash: string; size: number; contentType: string; uploadedBy: string; uploadedAt: number; deletedAt: number | null; }

#### NewBlobAttachment

{ id: string; docSlug: string; blobName: string; hash: string; size: number; contentType: string; uploadedBy: string; uploadedAt: number; deletedAt?: number | null | undefined; }

#### InsertDocument

{ id: string; slug: string; title: string; createdBy: string; createdAt: number; updatedAt: number; state?: string | undefined; visibility?: string | undefined; versionCount?: number | undefined; labelsJson?: string | undefined; expiresAt?: number | null | undefined; eventSeqCounter?: number | undefined; bftF?: number | undefined; requiredApprovals?: number | undefined; approvalTimeoutMs?: number | undefined; }

#### SelectDocument

{ id: string; slug: string; title: string; state: string; createdBy: string; visibility: string; createdAt: number; updatedAt: number; versionCount: number; labelsJson: string; expiresAt: number | null; eventSeqCounter: number; bftF: number; requiredApprovals: number; approvalTimeoutMs: number; }

#### AgentSessionState

AgentSessionState

Session state machine: Idle - Open - Active - Closing - Closed  - Idle: Initial state, waiting for open() - Open: Backend initialization in progress (transient; transitions to Active) - Active: Ready for contributions via contribute() - Closing: Teardown in progress (mutex-protected) - Closed: Teardown complete, receipt emitted

#### BFTApprovalStatus

BFTApprovalStatus

BFT approval status.

### Variables

#### STATE_CHANGING_METHODS

ReadonlySet<string>

HTTP methods that cause state changes and trigger audit logging.

#### CONTENT_LIMITS

{ readonly maxDocumentSize: number; readonly maxPatchSize: number; readonly maxBatchSize: 50; readonly maxVersionsPerDocument: 1000; readonly maxDocumentsPerUser: 10000; readonly maxWebhooksPerUser: 20; readonly maxMergeSources: 10; }

Hard content and resource limits enforced across the LLMtxt API. These limits are immutable and apply to all users regardless of tier.

#### API_VERSION_REGISTRY

Readonly<Record<number, ApiVersionInfo>>

Registry of all supported API versions. Add an entry here when a new API version ships.

#### CURRENT_API_VERSION

1

Current default API version served for unversioned requests.

#### LATEST_API_VERSION

number

Latest supported API version (derived from registry).

#### VALID_LINK_TYPES

readonly ["references", "depends_on", "derived_from", "supersedes", "related"]

Valid types for cross-document links. Used when linking one document to another with semantic meaning.

#### COLLECTION_EXPORT_SEPARATOR

"\n\n"

Separator string used when exporting multiple documents from a collection. Documents are joined with double newlines for readability.

#### API_KEY_PREFIX

"llmtxt_"

Prefix for API keys generated by the system. Keys take the form: llmtxt_43 chars base64url = 50 chars total

#### API_KEY_LENGTH

50

Total length of a valid API key including prefix. Calculated as: 7 (prefix) + 43 (base64url of 32 random bytes) = 50

#### API_KEY_DISPLAY_LENGTH

15

Length of the display prefix for API keys. Shows the first 8 random characters after the prefix for user identification.

#### ROLE_PERMISSIONS

Readonly<Record<DocumentRole, readonly Permission[]>>

Permission matrix for document roles. Mirrors the ROLE_PERMISSIONS constant from the Rust core — exported here so TypeScript consumers do not need to call the WASM `rolePermissions` helper for static look-ups.

#### MODEL_DIMS

384

Output dimensionality of the bundled model.

#### PROVIDER_NAME

"local-onnx-minilm-l6"

Provider identifier used in database records.

#### MODEL_NAME

"all-MiniLM-L6-v2"

Model name stored in database records.

#### jsonFormatSchema

z.ZodUnion<readonly [z.ZodRecord<z.ZodString, z.ZodUnknown>, z.ZodArray<z.ZodUnknown>, z.ZodString, z.ZodNumber, z.ZodBoolean, z.ZodNull]>

Schema that accepts any valid JSON value.

Matches objects, arrays, strings, numbers, booleans, and null. Used as the baseline structural check before optional predefined-schema enforcement.

#### textFormatSchema

z.ZodString

Schema that accepts any string value (plain text or markdown).

No structural constraints beyond being a string. Used by `validateText` in the validation layer.

#### markdownFormatSchema

z.ZodString

Schema for markdown content, stored as a plain string.

Functionally identical to `textFormatSchema`. Exists as a distinct export so callers can signal intent when working with markdown-specific pipelines.

#### promptMessageSchema

z.ZodObject<{ role: z.ZodEnum<{ system: "system"; user: "user"; assistant: "assistant"; }>; content: z.ZodString; }, z.core.$strip>

Schema for a single message within an LLM prompt conversation.

Validates that each message has a `role` (system, user, or assistant) and a `content` string, matching the format used by OpenAI and Anthropic APIs.

#### promptV1Schema

z.ZodObject<{ system: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; messages: z.ZodArray<z.ZodObject<{ role: z.ZodEnum<{ system: "system"; user: "user"; assistant: "assistant"; }>; content: z.ZodString; }, z.core.$strip>>; temperature: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodNumber>; max_tokens: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodNumber>; }, z.core.$strip>

Schema for the standard LLM prompt format (OpenAI / Anthropic style).

Validates an object with an optional `system` prompt, a non-empty `messages` array of `promptMessageSchema` entries, and optional `temperature` (0-2) and `max_tokens` (positive integer) fields.

#### predefinedSchemas

{ readonly 'prompt-v1': z.ZodObject<{ system: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; messages: z.ZodArray<z.ZodObject<{ role: z.ZodEnum<{ system: "system"; user: "user"; assistant: "assistant"; }>; content: z.ZodString; }, z.core.$strip>>; temperature: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodNumber>; max_tokens: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodNumber>; }, z.core.$strip>; }

Registry of predefined content schemas keyed by name.

Used by the validation layer to look up schemas by string name at runtime. Extend this object to register additional predefined formats.

#### compressRequestSchema

z.ZodObject<{ content: z.ZodString; format: z.ZodDefault<z.ZodOptional<z.ZodEnum<{ text: "text"; markdown: "markdown"; json: "json"; }>>>; schema: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; metadata: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodRecord<z.ZodString, z.ZodUnknown>>; }, z.core.$strip>

Schema for incoming content compression requests.

Validates that the request includes non-empty `content` and an optional `format` (defaults to `"text"`), optional `schema` name for predefined validation, and optional arbitrary `metadata`.

#### decompressRequestSchema

z.ZodObject<{ slug: z.ZodString; }, z.core.$strip>

Schema for incoming content decompression requests.

Validates that the request includes a non-empty `slug` identifying the stored document to decompress.

#### searchRequestSchema

z.ZodObject<{ query: z.ZodString; slugs: z.ZodArray<z.ZodString>; }, z.core.$strip>

Schema for incoming content search requests.

Validates that the request includes a non-empty `query` string and at least one document `slug` to search within.

#### DEFAULT_MAX_CONTENT_BYTES

number

Default maximum content size in bytes (5 MB).

#### DEFAULT_MAX_LINE_BYTES

number

Default maximum line length in bytes (64 KB).

#### DOCUMENT_STATES

readonly DocumentState[]

All valid document states in lifecycle order.

#### DEFAULT_APPROVAL_POLICY

ApprovalPolicy

Default approval policy: 1 approval, no timeout.

#### standaloneConfigSchema

z.ZodObject<{ topology: z.ZodLiteral<"standalone">; storagePath: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; identityPath: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; crsqlite: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodBoolean>; crsqliteExtPath: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; }, z.core.$strip>

Zod schema for `StandaloneConfig`.

#### hubSpokeConfigSchema

z.ZodObject<{ topology: z.ZodLiteral<"hub-spoke">; hubUrl: z.ZodString; apiKey: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; identityPath: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; persistLocally: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodBoolean>; storagePath: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; }, z.core.$strip>

Zod schema for `HubSpokeConfig`.

Zod validates the shape; `validateTopologyConfig` performs the cross-field business rules (e.g. persistLocally=true requires storagePath).

#### meshConfigSchema

z.ZodObject<{ topology: z.ZodLiteral<"mesh">; storagePath: z.ZodString; identityPath: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; peers: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodArray<z.ZodString>>; meshDir: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; transport: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodEnum<{ unix: "unix"; http: "http"; }>>; port: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodNumber>; }, z.core.$strip>

Zod schema for `MeshConfig`.

#### topologyConfigSchema

z.ZodDiscriminatedUnion<[z.ZodObject<{ topology: z.ZodLiteral<"standalone">; storagePath: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; identityPath: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; crsqlite: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodBoolean>; crsqliteExtPath: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; }, z.core.$strip>, z.ZodObject<{ topology: z.ZodLiteral<"hub-spoke">; hubUrl: z.ZodString; apiKey: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; identityPath: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; persistLocally: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodBoolean>; storagePath: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; }, z.core.$strip>, z.ZodObject<{ topology: z.ZodLiteral<"mesh">; storagePath: z.ZodString; identityPath: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; peers: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodArray<z.ZodString>>; meshDir: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; transport: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodEnum<{ unix: "unix"; http: "http"; }>>; port: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodNumber>; }, z.core.$strip>], "topology">

Discriminated-union Zod schema for any `TopologyConfig`.

Uses `z.discriminatedUnion` on the `topology` field for efficient parsing and precise field-level error messages.

#### FORMAT_EXT

Record<ExportFormat, string>

Map from export format to file extension.

#### FORMAT_CONTENT_TYPE

Record<ExportFormat, string>

Map from export format to HTTP Content-Type.

#### documents

import("drizzle-orm/sqlite-core").SQLiteTableWithColumns<{ name: "documents"; schema: undefined; columns: { id: import("drizzle-orm/sqlite-core").SQLiteColumn<{ name: string; tableName: "documents"; dataType: "string"; Documents table. The primary entity for the LocalBackend. Each document has a unique slug used in URLs and CRDT operations.

Versions table. Each row represents one version of a document. Incremental patches are stored alongside full content snapshots. Large content ( 10 KB) is written to the filesystem; content_ref stores the relative path.

#### sectionEmbeddings

notNull: true; hasDefault: false; isPrimaryKey: false; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: undefined; baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; generated: undefined; }, {}>; modelId: import("drizzle-orm/sqlite-core").SQLiteColumn<{ name: string; tableName: "section_embeddings"; dataType: "string"; data: string; driverParam: string; notNull: true; hasDefault: false; isPrimaryKey: false; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: [string, ...string[]]; data: number; driverParam: number; notNull: true; hasDefault: false; isPrimaryKey: false; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: undefined; baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; generated: undefined; }, {}>; }; dialect: "sqlite"; }>

Semantic embedding vectors for documents.  Unlike Postgres (which uses pgvector), LocalBackend stores vectors as raw Float32Array serialized to Buffers. Cosine similarity is computed in-memory via llmtxt-core WASM (similarity.rs). Acceptable for corpora up to ~10 000 documents.

#### blobAttachments

hasDefault: false; isPrimaryKey: false; isAutoincrement: false; hasRuntimeDefault: false; enumValues: undefined; baseColumn: never; identity: undefined; generated: undefined; }, {}>; }; dialect: "sqlite"; }>

Blob attachments table for LocalBackend.  Each row represents one attachment of a binary blob to a document. Bytes are stored on disk at /blobs/. LWW semantics: only one active record per (doc_slug, blob_name). Soft-delete: deleted_at non-null = detached.

#### ALLOWED_URI_REGEXP

RegExp

URI allowlist: only permit http:, https:, mailto:, and relative URIs. This blocks javascript:, data:, vbscript:, and other dangerous schemes.  The regex matches from the start of the URI value and allows: - http:// and https:// URIs - mailto: URIs - Relative URIs (starting with /, ./, ../  or a letter that is not a   protocol scheme character)  It denies any URI starting with a protocol label (word+colon) that is not explicitly http, https, or mailto.

#### ALLOWED_TAGS

readonly ["h1", "h2", "h3", "h4", "h5", "h6", "p", "br", "hr", "ul", "ol", "li", "blockquote", "pre", "code", "strong", "em", "b", "i", "u", "s", "del", "ins", "a", "table", "thead", "tbody", "tr", "th", "td", "div", "span", "img"]

Allowed HTML elements for markdown-rendered content. Deliberately excludes: script, style, iframe, object, embed, form, input, button.

#### ALLOWED_ATTR

readonly ["href", "title", "target", "rel", "src", "alt", "width", "height", "class", "id", "style"]

Allowed HTML attributes. Excludes all event handler attributes (on*).

#### FORBIDDEN_ATTR

readonly ["onerror", "onload", "onclick", "onmouseover", "onfocus", "onblur", "onchange", "oninput", "onkeydown", "onkeyup", "onkeypress", "onmousedown", "onmouseup", "onmousemove", "onmouseout", "onsubmit", "onreset", "onselect", "onscroll", "ondblclick", "oncontextmenu", "ondrag", "ondrop", "onpaste", "oncopy", "oncut", "onpointerdown", "onpointerup", "onpointermove", "onpointercancel", "onpointerover", "onpointerout", "onpointerenter", "onpointerleave", "onbegin", "onend", "onrepeat", "ondomcontentloaded", "onreadystatechange"]

Forbidden event handler attributes (comprehensive list covering OWASP cheat sheet payloads and SVG-specific handlers).

#### FORBIDDEN_CONTENTS

readonly ["script", "style", "iframe", "object", "embed", "form", "input", "button"]

Elements whose content must be stripped entirely (not just the tag). This prevents payloads like `<script>alert(1)</script>` from appearing as text content even after the script tag is removed.

#### MAX_CHANGESET_BYTES

number

Maximum changeset size: 10 MB (P3 spec §10).

#### MAX_RETRIES

3

Number of retry attempts before giving up.

#### RETRY_BASE_MS

1000

Base backoff delay in milliseconds (doubles each retry).

#### PG_AVAILABLE

boolean

True when DATABASE_URL_PG env var is set and the PG suite should run.

#### PEER_TTL_MS

number

Peer TTL in milliseconds. Peer files older than this and whose host is unreachable will be excluded (stale detection). Default: 5 minutes (300_000 ms).

#### AgentSessionState

{ Idle: "Idle"; Open: "Open"; Active: "Active"; Closing: "Closing"; Closed: "Closed"; }
